reviews.comment: Professor Adofo can be hard to understand, but I think he is a great professor who cares about student success. He defintiely overcomplicates his feedback but if you show up and are attentive and contribute during class he appreciates it and factors it into your grade. Just show that you care and he'll reward you.

I took capstone seminar and research and the way he instructed us to write was completely different. Class time didn't adequately prepare students to complete assignments. Instructor spent time in class asking students for their "concerns" about assignments he hadn't properly explained. I'd recommend to just take the class online.
Desmond we know you are self promoting... worst class i've ever taken. only good thing was the motivation points.

Even though it took him a while to grade things, he always left plenty of feedback. He then gives everyone the chance to resubmit and try for a better grade. I once resubmitted an assignment 7 times to fix the tiniest mistakes and he appreciated my efforts so much he gave me an A+. Trust me. You want to take this class.

MOTIVATION POINTS!! even if I did bad on an assignment, motivation points are Mr. Adofo's way of helping you out. If you seemed even semi interested during class, he would boost you from a C to an A. trust me. The work is so easy. You need to take this class.

Mr. Adofo put a lot of effort into making sure we understood every lesson and assignment. While his accent can be thick at times, he noticed when students were confused and kept explaining until we understood. The only way someone would leave the classroom not knowing smthng was if they just nodded to end the explanation—and that's not his fault.

Although some classes can seem boring, Mr. Adofo gave us time to celebrate our hard work and dedication when time allowed it. With our convincing, he even allowed us to have a free period where we played volleyball and bonded as a class. He truly cares about his students and does want us to have as much fun (while still being academic) as possible.

Despite the multiple bad reviews, Mr. Adofo's class is definitely worth taking. He listens to concerns, adjusts deadlines to the best of his ability, offers extra credit through motivation points, and allows resubmissions for a better grade. With effort and attention to feedback, students can genuinely succeed and enjoy the class.

Waits until after the last class of the semester to grade 3 out of the 4 projects. He never cancels class, even though all other professors constantly cancel, and doesn't grade any of the in-class assignments. He blames us for his own problems with management and asks for praise when he does his job. Grades based on how much he likes you.

By far one of the worst professors and human beings on planet earth. In my other writing-heavy class, I have an A, but I could be failing in this one. He can not articulate any of his thoughts. His class is truly unbearable. I hated every second of every day. He assigned so many pointless projects. You don't receive any feedback at all. Avoid him!

I took Professor Adofo's class in the summer. I thought he was a great teacher. He trys to help you get the best grade possible with manditory one on one meetings with him. You just have to ask questions and clarify things with him. He does have a pretty thick accicent. He also takes all semester to grade. Overall though he is a good teacher.

This class is one of the worst classes I've ever taken in my life. Mr. Adofo is a very nice person but he should not be a teacher. There is an obvious communication barrier between him and his students which is apparent when he goes to explain the assignments. He never fully understands the question you are asking him. Avoid taking his class.

Avoid this guy at all costs. Other professors only have the 4 required papers but this man has discussion posts and presentations every class on top of the 4 papers. I spent more time reading the textbook, making presentations, writing discussion posts, and not to mention writing the 1500+ word papers than studying for my literal Calculus II class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Mr. Adofo is a great person, although I did not enjoy his class. He takes all semester to grade, when I finally got my grade and feedback for each project I already turned everything in for the class. He pointed out very minor issues even if you had everything on the rubric. Lots of group work, I am not sure he even graded or not. Do not take!

Horrible. Heavy accent, extremely unclear with instructions, constantly mumbles to himself and trails off. Made questionable remarks towards his students. Grades like his laptop is ancient. Bro literally started grading all of my projects the night before grades are due. Gives students "motivational points" when its just pity points. Horrendous.

It's as if he's never used a computer before, he does an awful job of listing assignments in canvas, communicating deadlines, or responding to emails. Because of a general lack of communication, his class felt extremely disorganized, and I didn't feel like this class taught me anything meaningful in any way.

The class was extremely unorganized. Although he showed knowledge of the subject, he had a very difficult time communicating deadlines and assignments. He also gave everyone the same grade on each project and did not update his Canvas modules after Week 3. This class almost felt like a waste of time, but he's nice.

Dr. Maciel is an amazing professor! His lectures are so interesting and you can tell he really has a passion for teaching. He has a strict no-phone and handwritten notes policy, but it's not that bad. You really learn and get to know your classmates. Gives multiple chances for extra credit, and the test are simple if you study.
He has a no-phone policy and you must take hand-written notes. There is also a large class project that is cumulative throughout the semester. He does care for his students though!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Maciel is very knowledgeable and a great guy! He has a few policies (no electronic devices, mandatory attendance), but I honestly didn't mind them. His lectures were very interesting, and you can tell he knows what he's talking about. There are a couple of homework assignments, 3 tests, and a big group project, but it's all pretty interesting.
Loved her and this class
Such a sweet and brilliant professor! She is very understanding and always tries her best to engage the class. I hope to be able to take another one of her classes during my academic journey. :)

This class in itself was HARD material, but she made it so easy to understand and inquire about. I found myself loving the activities, and the lectures were so interesting once you get into the material. She is incredibly accommodating, and genuinely one of the nicest people I've ever met-- looking to take another class from her!!
The only reason I am taking this professor again is because I have to take his micro class.

He doesn't post his powerpoints until after he's covered them so get ready to take notes. Know how to do all the homeworks and examples/in class assessments because they will show up on the test. Tests are low # of questions. A little hard to study for if you don't know what you're doing but he'll help you if you ask. Labs are easy cause he helps.

Has industry experience and knows his stuff. Only downside is that he talks quietly so make sure to sit in the front of the class. Participation and answering questions will result in bonus points. In-class assignments are given.

What a professor. Honestly the best professor Ive ever taken. He is the type of professor you would want to take all your classes with. Dont listen to anyone that says he never is in his office hours. He keeps his door closed but all you have to do is knock and he will tell you to come in right away. Final is open note open book take home.

BEST PROFESSOR BY FAR, no matter what the subject is he makes it fun and engaging. It is impossible to fail his class since he gives extra credit and gives a lot of partial credit during class when going over the exam. Final exam is a open book and note take home exam. You will learn from just listening to him teach. Amazing teacher .10/10

Funny, and nice, and, but he can not help you understand any thing related to structure. He is unable to answer questions related to course material. If you registered to this instructor you need to learn on your own. His office hours is a big lie.

Being in his course is a waste of time. When asked questions, he avoids answering and redirects to irrelevant conversations. He pushes students to find help on websites he can't assist with. His office hours are nonexistent, he is never available, never responds to emails.

There is no meaningful lecture, only PowerPoints and videos. He is incapable of providing any help and is unavailable during office hours. Unfortunately, when I had to take the course, he was the only professor assigned. Recently, UNF hired a highly knowledgeable professor for CES3605, which highlights the deficiencies in his teaching.

He is unable to assist with structural calculations or analysis and shows a lack of understanding of the course material in civil engineering concepts. His reliance on offering excessive extra credit to compensate for his inability to teach engineering materials. Don't recommend him for any engineering courses. It's need urgent attention from UNF.

I LOVE EL SAFTY!!! its clear he cares about his students and their education. He wants everyone to do good and become good engineers. Going to his office hours feels very welcoming and helps with things you don't understand.

Few opportunities for feedback. Terrible lectures and use of class time. It is absolute chaos and he encourages people to shout out answers. He teaches very little and frequently cancels class. He WILL pass you - gives extra credit all the time. But if you're here to learn, don't take this professor.
El Safty is a great professor. The best instructor ever!
Man, El-Safty is the best! Experienced, Very clear, very funny. He makes sure that you are taken care of.
Amazing professor. The best teacher I ever had!

This Professor doesnt teach all that much. Mostly has everything automated on Canvas. Most lectures are old videos posted onto Canvas, but what he goes over isnt tested on. He requires you to go into the book and learn all of the harder equations. No point in having him as a professor since youre more likely to be teaching yourself.
Dr. Adel is the best professor at UNF. his teaching style is intersest and useful. he is helpful and justice.
Dr. Adel is the best professor at UNF. He is sincere and devoted to his work. he is so helpful for students.
Dr. Elsafty is the man. Without him there will be no Civil engineering in this campus.
Super!!!!!
Dr. Elsafty is a very experienced Engineer and talented Professor. Take his class! You will learn a lot !
No Comments
Man, He is the best! Experienced, Very clear, very funny. he is basically doing every thing in the department and he makes sure that you are taken care of.

He's the founder of applied statistics in FSU Stats department. As always, aged professor always mind their participations. This is a graduate course that been taught by him at least 20 times. Reasonable grading and really care about student behavior. Somehow, I believe if he give a close notes exam. It's going to be tough cuz he's knowledgable.

Wow this professor was not good. He doesn't give study guides, attendance is mandatory but not part of your grade, he doesn't communicate effectively and is entirely unengaging. The lectures were incredibly boring and the content ramped up with no warning. It was just downright awful, don't take him if you value your sanity at all.

Teaching wise hes okay, gets off topic a lot, talks about his son too much iykyk. Overall just really negative and discouraging, calls on people who he knows will get the question wrong to embarrass them, etc. As much as I hated the environment, attendance is required and it was the worst part of my day every time I went. Grading was good though.
She did a great job at posting weekly with cool videos that were edited by the EMHS team.

Dr. Audrey Casserleigh's course on 9/11 has been by far the most engaging and memorable class I've taken. She is caring, funny, understanding, and truly invested in her students' success. After 4 years at FSU, she is the one professor I will always remember, along with this impactful course.

I took her 9/11 class this semester, and I loved it! Professor Casserleigh reminds me of Mrs. Frizzle in the best possible way. She broke down the information we were learning into concepts that were both understandable and engaging. The class is structured like a book club, and it is not hard to earn an A. I highly recommend taking it.

This class is so much fun but I am taking it in person so it may be slightly different if you take it online. Gives so many extra credit opportunities. 2 exams (which the class makes half of the questions) and one paper but if you pace yourself it is easy.

If you take the Online Course, she does not even go through the slides with you; she just expects you to go through them yourself without her input on the information. Expects you to buy HER book for like 200$. She is very lacking in communication, and I wouldn't take the online version again. It is probably best to take the in-person version.

Casserleigh is an okay professor, if you NEVER have to email her for anything at all. She is horrible with emails, will take 3+ weeks to respond, or also quite often just not respond at all. She also has an ego issue and will take anything and everything you say as a personal offense, so be very careful with how you word things with her.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: One of my absolue favorite professors at FSU. I am thankful I was able to learn from Professor Casserleigh

Dr. Casserleigh and Kate are incredible, best class I have taken at FSU so far. They are both incredibly passionate about their work and Dr. Casserleigh is hilarious, incredibly kind, and a really interesting person to chat with. 2 Exams and a Paper so make sure to produce high quality work and study hard, read the text and you'll get an A easily.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

reviews.comment: extremely engaging and interesting content. Book isn't necessary, but extremely helpful for the tests.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Casserleigh & her TA Kate were amazing. They are really knowledgeable about the subject to the point where every lecture just blew me away. They got this ish down to a science and this class is 100% worth taking, especially in person. You're gonna have to grind for the 3 exams & the final paper was not a walk in the park, but nothing horrid.

I took Introduction to Terrorism: Preparedness and Response with Professor Casserleigh. There are 3 exams and 2 papers. The exams are open notes. The slides and video lectures are very engaging. The professor is knowledgeable and makes the subject interesting. This class is a great elective choice as long as you don't mind writing and 3 exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I loved this course and Casserleigh. She clearly understands the subject material, and keeps class entertaining. While your grade is only made up of a few things, it is still a very doable course. She encourages students not to buy the textbook, and instead find an online version for free. There are 2 reading quizzes, 2 papers, and 3 tests.
great lectures and super passionate

my favorite prof at fsu she brings her dog into class and she is genuinely passionate about such a bleak topic but she makes every lecture so funny and I've really enjoyed being in her class. the assignments aren't too hard just make sure you read her book (a little pricey)
Hands down best teacher at FSU. She cares deeply about her students. Easy class and she is very understandable. I wish I took her classes sooner

I would definitely recommend taking this class. It is not very homework-heavy and the tests are open-book but I would say the hardest thing is her reading quiz. Do not underestimate that quiz she goes into specific details instead of being broad as well as reading the articles she assigns. Other than that this class was fairly easy.

This is my second class with Dr. Casserleigh and she's a fantastic teacher that takes the time to get to know her students. She has students lead the conversations with her guidance and shows a lot of support as a teacher and as a mentor. Her activities are very engaging, too. Amazing Professor, I strongly urge you to take her.

There is not enough words to describe how amazing this professor is. I have taken about 4 classes with her and she is so knowledgeable and cares about her students. TAKE HER CLASS YOU WILL NOT REGRET IT!!! SHES AMAZING
Professor Casserleigh was great! She's a very caring professor that's very responsive and respectful, and most importantly knowledgeable in her field.
The textbook is a must but there are 3 exams (all open notes and textbook) as well as two easy papers. She is super kind and the topic is interesting! Super easy class.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: This class was amazing! I found the topic to be very interesting and it made it so much better that our professor was great. She gives EC opportunities for every exam, all exams and quizzes are open book and note. Her powerpoints are very thorough and easy to understand. If you want a fun class that isn't going to stress you out I highly recommend!
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Dr. Casserleigh is my favorite professor at FSU! She is so knowledgeable about the topic (she wrote the textbook), and she makes the lectures fun and easy to understand. She cares a lot about her students and wants them to succeed; I would take every class this woman teaches if it were my major

I don't think I have ever loved a professor more. She is amazing at teaching and lectures still couldn't be boring if the material was bland. 11/10 recommend. Class is an easy A tbh. 2 reading quizzes- literally straight from her book. 3 exams that come straight from the PowerPoints she gives you. 2 small papers and you pick who to talk about.

Dr. Casserleigh is the best professor I've had at Florida State University. I would highly recommend taking her classes in person (or at least on zoom). She wont hesitate to tell you how it really is. It's not hard to get on her good side, just do the work and speak up in class. You only need the book (use the PDF) for the reading quizzes.

She is hilarious and such a caring professor! Took an online class and consisted of 2 short papers, 3 exams based on powerpoints, and the textbook. The only downside is that you need the textbook (which is expensive), but overall she is the best!

Dr. Casserleigh is my favorite professor at FSU. She is hilarious which makes the lectures engaging especially since it is a heavier topic. She really wants to help her students. Do the study guides because the exams will be pretty much word for word from it! The 2 reading quizzes are hard if you don't read, but everything else will even it out.

I've never bothered to review anyone before, but this class needs it. Prof. Casserleigh, even online, comes off as short with students. She doesn't seem to use her own rubrics when grading, and she gives test questions on things that she previously said weren't testable. Honestly she's made this one of my least favorite learning experiences @ FSU.

By far one of my favorite professors I've ever taken. She keeps lectures interesting and talks a lot from personal experience in the field. Very knowledgeable, very caring about her students: offers study guides for the exams, available to talk about current events or otherwise outside of class. Take her!

This is, by far, the best professor that I have taken at FSU. Her lectures make dreary and dark topics interesting and understandable. Her humor is probably the best in the department, if not in the entire school. Definitely get to know her in office hours. Her reading quizzes are challenging to say the least - read the book and take notes on it.

Honestly this is the BEST professor I've had at FSU. She knows what she's taking about through experience. Her lectures are super interesting and jam packed with information. I wouldn't say her class is an easy A but if you give at least 70% of effort you will get an A. I cannot stress this enough, DO THE STUDY GUIDES AND GO TO OFFICE HOURS!!!!

She is easily the funniest professors I've ever had. That being said, she really wants everyone to succeed. Use the study guide for exams. You must read the text for the reading quizzes but she tries to make it as accessible as possible. I genuinely enjoy her class and I learn a lot while i'm there.

My absolute favorite professor on this campus. She has an amazing way of being able to explain, in minute detail, the content without making it go over your head. Her lectures are fantastic, she gives AMAZING feedback, and prepares students well for her tests. The one downside - reading quizzes. But she'll answer any and all questions you have!

Prof. Casserleigh is an amazing professor and really knows her stuff. Inside the classroom she is so funny and makes her lectures very entertaining. With that said, talking to her outside of the classroom is a nightmare. She is not nice and shows a lot of favoritism. Also be prepared to buy the$200 textbook that she wrote or you will fail the quiz

She's honestly one of the best professors I've ever had. She has so much knowledge and she explains it in a way that everyone understands it. She's a bit biased when explaining things but it didn't bother me as I'm a "2 sids to every story " type of person. Her tests are HARD, you have HAVE to got class and also she's a tough grader but shes great

Horribly unprofessional. You ask questions through email, and she takes it as you are questioning her teaching. She also says she is "generous" for answering your questions. She gives fill-in-the-blank exams online that are absurdly graded, and unnecessarily vague in the way the question is asked. I simply cared about my grade, but she was so rude.

One of the Best Professors a college could have. You learn in all of her classes. She is definitely concerned about ALL OF HER STUDENTS. As long as you care. Fair to EVERYONE. DOES NOT DISCRIMINATE IN ANY ARENA
The single greatest teacher at FSU. No contest. One of those amazing teachers youll actually remember when you look back on your academic career.

I have taken her classes online and on campus. She is amazing! Online, make sure you know the information as well as have the book, readings, and powerpoints handy. This is not a professor that you can just do the minimum with. You need to work. She is extremely knowledgeable and has a great personality. My favorite prof @ FSU by far!

Finishing up my third class with her and I have loved all 3 classes. She is a great teacher. She really gets to know the students who show up to class and makes class fun for them. I would highly recommend anything she teaches. So funny. One of my favorites at FSU.
I absolutely loved her class this semester and you should absolutely take her classes. One of the best.

Audrey is the sweetest professor! Took her online for Terrorism. 2 response papers, a scavenger hunt assignment and Weekly lecture video powerpoints and typed powerpoints. TA's very helpful also and willing to give feedback for any assignment before you turn it in. Definitely recommend this class!
One of my favorite professors. Makes the class enjoyable and easy to understand. She is absolutely hilarious and relatable.

Dr. Casserleigh is one of my favorite teachers at FSU. Her lectures are extremely interesting and she is very engaging. I learned more than I thought I would. If you go to class, pay attention, and actually study the material, you can get an A. 10/10 would recommend.
Super smart - great online lectures.  I only wish more of the material was lecture based bc she is so good at it.
Do the extra credit and you'll get an A

Took the class online, so participation is a thing. Also, you need to read the weekly assignments, but it's usually just about 10 pages, so not a big deal. It's a slow paced class, so it's good to take if you want a full schedule. She's kinda funny, in a cheesy way tho. lol Exams aren't hard. Especially if you read the 10 pages a week assignments.

You have weekly discussion board posts and must comment on other students essays. You get into a routine with the work so you always know when your assignments are due. 2 tests, 2 quizzes and 1 paper. Read all the power points and readings and you should get an A on the tests and quizzes. The essay wasn't too harshly graded. Overall, easy-ish.
Great Teacher, loves her job and the entire team works together wonderfully. She outsources her grading to Brad who is a strict grader. All in all great class and classes.
Awesome class - lots of great discussion. She does let the class get off track of students want to talk about something.

Audrey is a phenomenal professor. She is one of the few professors I will actually take at FSU (as opposed to grad students). She is available early in the morning and until later in the afternoon. She is brutally honest, but is also always willing to help. She is well versed in her field and is knowledgeable, well beyond the level of many others.

Dr. Casserleigh (or Miss Frizzle as she likes to be called) is funny, engaging, and 100% honest. When she's there, the class is interesting and there is lots of discussion. Unfortunately, she is often on business trips that take her away from the classroom.

Intro to terrorism is literally my only C in college. STAY AWAY from it online! She is not helpful at all and all the tests are 50 questions and you only have an hour. Even reading all the material i would get 80's on the tests. DO NOT TAKE....

Great teacher, she really puts the effort in and helps make the material understandable and easy to retain. She is very enthusiastic and works really hard to help each individual student, TAKE THIS TEACHER SHE IS AWESOME!
Avoid taking the On line version class. No instruction from Professor...all contact by Mentor. Regret taking the course.

She doesn't provide the lecture powerpoints until class is already done. She does her powerpoints in a different order than the book itself and can't clarify which section of the book we are in. When she reads powerpoints, she doesn't add much else input and just reads what is on the screen. Her manner is quite unwelcoming.

She was good but I felt that she graded our tests unnecessarily harshly. I could understand the lecture perfectly and write down my response on the test knowing that my answer was correct and she would pick it apart and claim otherwise. She is VERY picky on tests. You get an attendance grade for participation and coming to class, so arrive on time.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Minniefield is a breath of fresh air as a professor. She is insanely intelligent and I absolutely love the way she grades and gives feedback. I'm not a fan of the groupwork we had in this course, but I see where it benefitted me. You have to work hard to get a good grade in her class. I love her as a person and professor and think she is great!
reviews.rating_tags: Group projects--Graded by few things--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Dr. Minniefield is a very competent and empathetic professor who knows her field very well. However, she often sticks to the script with PowerPoint slides too rigidly and does not bother writing on the whiteboard. Outside lectures, she can be accommodating. What I love about taking this course is the verbose exams. I enjoyed every moment of it.

This professor made what should have been a fairly simple class incredibly challenging. Her lectures have too much information and are hard to understand (other students explained the material better). Most of the grades are tests, but the questions are strange and studying the lecture material doesn't guarantee success. She's a nice prof, just new
She is so considerate and understanding. She makes the best jokes which allows her students to feel very comfortable in the classroom. She is such an amazing teacher, I would highly recommend.

Genuinely the easiest class I've ever taken. No exams, just weekly at-home quizzes, so take that as you will. Attendance is 10% of grade. Recitation was just as easy. If you can't get an A in this class you should just drop out. Nice professor too and gives extensions.

Literally the easiest stats class ever, no exams or final and just online canvas quizzes you do outside of class. Attendance not mandatory but graded for 1 point. Will give extensions, super nice and understanding.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: She made it a very easy class. Weekly lectures with attendance for points but just went through guided notes every class and was super understanding. Would take again.

She is amazing! Easiest class ever, takes in consideration outside forces and is very easy to talk to and work with. All quizzes are on canvas and there is no final cumulative exam. Make a GroupMe for the class to collaborate. I love the class so easy.

Radha was the sweetest professor ever. Her quizzes were super easy if you use the notes and attendance isn't mandatory. She even rounded my 89.4 up to an A for me. Overall a very nice and understanding professor.

This class was so easy and I have never taken Statistics before. There are no exams just a quiz every week that you take at home. The lab on Fridays was very easy you just talk with a group about the topics and what you don't understand. No final exam. There's an attendance question at the end of each lecture but it doesn't get graded on accuracy.

This was a very easy class. As long as you kept up with lectures its a easy A. Only online quizes with no honorlocke. The labs were fairly easy too usually 3 questions and all you had to do was show the TA you did them.

attendance to lectures are not mandatory except for the discussions once a week. the only assignments given are online quizzes that are open for 24+ hours, the attendance for discussions, with the lowest 2 discs omitted from the final grade, and the LSQA (mandatory to pass). you can make up quizzes and discussions with full credit as well.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Easiest math class at Florida State. No exams, only quizzes that are easy and a lab on Friday. participation isn't mandatory at all for the lecture.

Highly recommend!! Weekly online Canvas quizzes no honorlock. Attendance is not mandatory and all notes are posted on Canvas. She is very caring and goes slow to make sure everyone understands. Recitations are once a week with a TA (mandatory), work in small groups and if you miss you can go to office hours to get credit back. No final!!

Nice professor and goes over work slowly but lectures are slightly confusing and her handwriting is impossible to read. I am just genuinely bad at math so I found it hard. But she offers office hours to help. Weekly quizzes, no honor lock, and mandatory meetings with TAs once a week.

She has every week online canvas quizzes. She never uses honorlock. Attendance is not mandatory but her lectures can be confusing. She try's to make sure you understand. She teaches slow so that everyone can follow. Notes can be accessible on canvas. Discussions with TA's are mandatory and they make you do problems on worksheets.
She does not answer questions although she promotes it, maybe  because it is a big class but the lack of interaction makes it easy to fall asleep to.
Great teacher. Quizzes and recitation every week.

She was by far the best math-related professor I've had. I took STA2122 and her lectures were extremely clear and concise and were perfect for someone who isn't the most number-savvy. She also managed to keep class lighthearted and I appreciated how available she was for meeting outside of class for questions etc. Definitely would take again.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Ms Bose is my fav math instructor ever. I registered for STA2122 in an earlier semester and had to drop bc the student "teaching' that class was HORRIBLE. But Ms. Bose made it so easy! She uses so many examples and explains in her videos. Watch/ go to the lectures & do the work and you'll do great! Unlimited tries for quizzes and 2 for tests!

Absolutely loved this class. Professor Bose is the sweetest lady and is very understanding. Lectures were easy to follow and quizzes directly reflected the material. As long as you pay attention, you'll pass with an A.

This class was a breeze, especially online. There was no final exam, no invasive proctoring software, and the weekly quizzes were extremely easy. The only thing to beware of are the extremely long, boring, and monotonous pre-recorded lectures. You can't skip these though, or you won't do well on the quizzes! Very easy, highly recommend.

Excellent professor. Only professor at FSU that offered sympathy/understanding when I had a covid issue during distance learning. Truly cares about students and could not recommend her more! Made the material digestible and was very fair with the quizzes. Always had bad math teachers until now, reminded me why learning it is fun again :)
The only issue I had with this class was the length of the lecture videos which were hours long. No final and you could take the quizzes multiple times for a better grade.

This woman is an angel on earth. Makes a tough subject easy yet useful at the same time. I talked with her before every quiz and she was happy to answer any questions. She respected me which made me respect her. Her lectures were easy to follow. The exams were straightforward and not difficult at all.

She is by far the best math teacher that I have had at FSU!! she really cares about her students and makes the material pretty easy to understand because she goes into detail on how to solve everything. In-person quizzes can be hard so do the try on your own problems in the notes bc that's the best way to study! overall a great teacher!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring

reviews.comment: take her take her take herrrr. cannot recommend her more she is SUCH a nice prof. math was alwayss a drag for me as its not my forte, & in hs i almost failed stats so i was deathly afraid for this class but it was required. i will most likely pass w an A/B+ bc she's awesome. study the packets & i found attending lectures really benefitted me.

Professor Bose is great. There are online quizzes, with unlimited attempts and are an easy A along with in class quizzes (ICQ). ICQ are a bit more difficult but the problems are straight from the notes SHE POSTS ONLINE FILLED OUT and you get a front and back cheat sheet. The material is not too bad and for the final she tells you what to study!

Great teacher, kinda boring lectures as we only get through about 2 pages of notes each lecture. Quizzes in person every other week and lets you use a pg of notes and 2 pages of notes on final.

reviews.comment: Disclaimer: I'm nowhere close to being a math person.I stopped going to class during the second week and still got an A... that's how easy this course is. There's no access code or textbook for this class, she provides all the notes needed which is nice. Very nice professor, gives you lots of opportunities to help your grade.

She provides many opportunities to save your grade. She explains the material clearly. So if you're not good at math, make sure to come to class. If you read and actually do the worksheets she provides, then you'll know exactly what's on the quizzes. She's surprisingly funny. She's also very approachable. A great teacher overall.
Great Professor enjoyed learning and really cared about students.
LOVE Radha

I think she is the absolute sweetest. She makes things very clear and does not hesitate to review any concepts the class has trouble with (which is rare). I suggest to print the notes and bring to class to follow along with her. She lets you have a cheat sheet for the quizzes every other week. Highly recommend & I'm not talented at math.

Super nice! Class is really easy. Gives you cheat sheets for the quizzes and the final. Just go to class and the material will be so easy. You might have a harder time though if you don't go to class. Tells you exactly what kind of questions will be on the final. Couldn't ask for an easier stats teacher and she is very nice and funny.

She's a really sweet teacher. Her lectures are very clear and helpful and she gives her students many opportunities to excel. The only reason you can fail is if you don't show up to her lectures or classes. I would recommend her to even the worst Math students! Ps. you can always go to her office hours for help!
She makes the class as easy as possible by explaining only what you need to know. Very helpful. Drops 2 lowest grades. No textbook, print the notes.
GREAT teacher. Very kind and very good teacher. She makes the class as easy as she can and is very good at explaining the material. Dont buy the book just print the notes and go to class.

Don't buy the book. Recitations were mandatory, however EXTREMELY helpful. They drop the lowest test grade, and 3 lowest recitation grades, and she allowed a full page, front and back note sheet on the test. Very easy class
Very easy class that is made easier by her. You don't even have to go to class if  you don't want to because tutoringzone can get you an A. She's not bad on the eyes either :)
Such an easy class if you regularly attend the class and the recitations. Excellent set up with the quizzes in recitations, 3 tests and the final which make it easy to get a good grade. Thanks Radha!

EASY!!!! Dont bother going to class or buying the book, just go to tutoringzone. Class isnt mandatory which is awesome because no one can understand her. The book is worthless. I recommend her just because she is so easy.
I <3 Radha
No Comments

His class was relaxed, and he was very kind. He provided excellent feedback on essays and drafts, which helped make the class manageable. He often let us leave early. While there was a lot of writing, that is expected in an English class. I would definitely recommend this course.
Noah keeps it so real. He makes expectations clear and grades fairly. He is so chill and funny. Definitely take him for english.

this class was so easy! at first i was scared because ITS ALOT OF ESSAYS but he made it easy to understand. he would give out alot of resources like sample essays, powerpoints, rubrics, etc. he is a very easy grader so it's such an easy A
Very good and down to earth professor
Best teacher ever.
easiest A class ive taken- don't let the mandatory attendance scare you!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--So many papers

reviews.comment: He is an amazing professor and provides great feedback.  He makes class so fun and has a great personality.  If you do your work efficiently in class he will let class out early.  He made english very enjoyable and helped me as a writer.  I would recommend anybody to take him who wants to have a good time and learn a lot! 10/10
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Noah Brown is an amazing professor. Project guidelines and grading criteria are very clear plus he posts many examples and resources.

Great professor and would highly recommend taking his course. Really creates a great environment where people can truly get interested in the subject matter they are writing on. I've hated English all my life up until his class.

Attendance isn't mandatory and ur grade is based on 3 exams (non cumulative) + online chapter quizzes (super easy). She gives a bunch of extra credit opportunities also and gives great lectures. I'd recommend going to class if you can she makes it easy to understand. Overall 10/10.
She made lectures easy to take notes on and study from. Also wrote me a letter of recommendation for graduate programs which was so sweet. Highly recommended

THIS DIVA. I loved her so so much. She made lectures interesting. So many extra credit opportunities. She does quick review questions during class that are similar to exam questions. I always felt supported by her in this class and she made the material very interesting.

Katie was the most amazing teacher. Though the slides very relatively easy, going to class made them seem even more easier. The tests aren't that bad either. Its cause of her I want to be involved more into research in psychology. She's very caring and will help you according to your situation.

Ms. Brousse was such a great teacher and made learning fun. It's an intro to psych class, so it's nothing too crazy. The professor is really nice and chill, and she's also pretty funny. There are 5 exams, including a final but if you go to class or at the very least just look at the lecture slides, you'll be fine.

Katie was really cool and laid back. Attendance wasn't mandatory and all her lectures are online so studying for exams was really easy. Class is based mainly on easy 4-question quizzes and 5 exams with the last 1 being optional. Only thing is to make sure you start research credits early and don't wait til the last minute. Great professor and class

Dr. Bosukonda is AWESOME. He ensures his students understand every part of the material, which makes this class very easy, as well as very informative. The group project has in-class work days almost every week. He gives a lot of guidance in class so make sure you show up!
Best professor ever. Super caring and understanding. Makes sure everyone understands everything before he moves on and the exams are super easy as he covers all the content so thoroughly.

He is smart and kind. Class consists of a few homework assignments that are started in class and usually easy, a few exams that are extremely easy because he posts practice exams that are super similar to the actual exam, and a final group project that he grades leniently. You can tell he wants to do a good job and wants his students to succeed.

IDC what anyone else says, I really really enjoyed the class! I took the course online, so I can just see the zoom video whenever I want. At the end of the lecture videos, he'll explain the exercise hw. Just time manage like hw/tests are due on Tuesdays. More understanding than most! Very helpful, but straightforward.
currently doing the capstone project and I've never been more confused. send help.
Professor Pelham is a wealth of knowledge and an interesting guy. I have enjoyed the few classes I've taken with him. He takes student feedback into consideration to make the courses better.

He literally gives you his personal phone number and says if you have issues with any assignment to call him. He cares very deeply about students doing well and is very knowledgeable about both criminology and computer literacy. His class is not hard at all if you read the textbooks and ask questions.

He comes off as very cold, but that's because he's a law enforcement officer. Once you've actually introduced yourself and gotten comfortable around him he's actually really nice. He's super respectful of his students and while the work is hard, he makes it easy to understand. The Capstone project is a mess and needs clearer instructions though.

I took him for CCJ4744 (it wasn't showing up as an option for this review) and he was absolutely awful. Instructions for assignments were ALWAYS unclear and confusing. He waited 4 weeks to grade my first 4 discussion posts, and gave me a 50% on each one because I didn't separate them into paragraphs, which was not in the instructions. RUN
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: So many assignments for an online class. Over 25 online modules. Rude and not helpful.

The work puts into practice what we have learned. He is very helpful and always reachable for students. He wants nothing more than to see his students succeed. His blackboard page is VERY organized. Assignments are easy and he generally allows a make up week at the end of the semester. Great guy with actual experience.

This professor is the best in the safety & security program. Professor Banyon assignments are straight forward and easy to understand. Reading quizzes are easy. If you pass your quizzes you sure will past his test. Unfortunately this my last sem. Time to graduate but he will go down in history as my favorite professor at FSU.
Wonderful. He isn't too tough but tough enough and will bend over backwards to help you. He clearly loves his job, knows his stuff and loves his students!!!!! Take him!!!!!
AWESOME!!!!!! Assignments not hard. Quizzes and test are moderate. His quizzes combined are his test. Professor Palhem does not grade tough.

Well respected and not only knows the material, but he will go out of his way to help you.  His assignments are very organized, but if you have problem, he will email you back in hours, if not minutes. He doesn't have a lot of that un-needed uppity "pride" like many professors do, he's just there to teach and he has fun with it.  Awesome teacher!

Professor Banyon Pelham excellent professor. Assignments are easy quizzes are open book which are very easy. If you need a computer credit I recommend this professor. The capstone project was easy just pay attention to the excel assignment and make sure you know it because it will resurface during the semester.

I have her for linear algebra and have never had a better math professor. Even when the energy in the room is dead she always has a welcoming smile and a positive energy that could make a flower bloom. Incredibly helpful.

AMAZING PROFESSOR! She was so good at explaining subjects in her YouTube lectures. Homework and tests matches what you learn. Watch her lectures, stay on track, study and easy A. The topics get a bit harder as one goes but that's just the class.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Had Cobb for Calc I and II. One of my favorite math professors, very consistent with expectations and preparation. Gave you things to look for on exams and tests were similar to problems already worked through. Very professional and teaches with a clear expertise. Amazing professor!
Best teacher I've taken at FSU. Is very understanding and reasonable. Wish every teacher would structure and organize their class as well as hers.

Dr. Cobb is known as the 'Math Queen". She'll go through the content until you understand it and will answer any and all questions you send her over email. I couldn't of asked for a better Calc I professor. Homework isn't required, but you really need to do it to get a good grade. Tests are basically the homeworks.

Why yall dogging on her? She is very straightforward w/ the work and prints out the directions for you. The labs are meant to b difficult progressively. Gives volunteer opportunities. If you don't understand the lecture, you'll understand the lab. Just turn it in time and you're good. Bro she even helps you in the labs. Just be on time to class...

Took this online and had a terrible TA. Would take off points for no reason and my grade would fluctuate constantly. Work was all busy work and a waste of time, mostly useless discussion boards. Quizzes were so specific and timed. Easy content but boring and unhelpful.

I have never met a more uncaring professor. The actual assignments are not hard, just incredibly useless and time consuming. 3-4 weekly assignments. Good luck emailing her if you encounter a problem as she will most likely be curt and condescending. Does not believe in extra credit,rounding,or extensions.

unkind and uncaring. I am disabled and have ended up in the hospital with documentation and approval from higher ups and she refuses to extend assignments on the basis that we had a week to do them. if she wanted them done on day 2 they should have been due day 2, not day 7. she discriminantes against disabled students, is a walking ADA violation.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: One of the rudest instructors I have ever had. Poor instruction, rude responses and nasty paragraphs galore.  The worst class I have ever taken. She in as rude as can be, and penalizes students for her mistakes: saying X in instructions then taking points off for X, and then claiming she never said X in the first place. AVOID AVOID AVOID.
If you forget to do an assignment, prepare for the worst, as this woman is very unforgiving when it comes to you getting credit on assignments.

Pretty easy and interesting class. Her lectures are pretty short but informative. Every week you have to read a chapter in the book and take a quiz. All quizzes and exams are open note. You have the occasional discussion post but other than that really easy and interesting class!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: This class was so much busy work for just an ethics credit. If you stay on top of your work you'll do fine. Tough grader and doesn't round. Very unapproachable and can be vague when describing what she expects in your work.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Exams had poor wording which made the questions misleading and the exams more difficult. Also if you get 4 questions wrong on an exam you already are getting a B on the exam. Absolutely useless. hands down worst professor i've ever had and is absolutely no help. it's like she wants to see you fail. Very unpleasant to talk to as well don't waste your time or money of charla
The class itself was not difficult. Perdue was passive aggressive in her announcements and emails. She was not very helpful. Her TAs were more help.

Super sweet! Her lectures are mostly visual-based; she shows a lot of videos and they are very engaging. Her quizzes and even exams are open book/open notes. As long as you put in effort to make notes from the textbook and do the work in a timely manner, you will succeed in the class. She makes the assignments available for the entire week.

She's incredibly sweet and is always willing to help you out if you reach out with questions or concerns. She does NOT accept late work but you have plenty of time to get your assignments done throughout the week. Her assignments are heavily based on the textbook but are still fun to complete. I enjoy her class very much and highly recommend her!

She is incredibly rude and makes the quiz wording weirdly to make up for such an easy class. Her quizzes try to trick you. I have never received a polite response from her. Do not take this class if you don't have to

I took her online class, and it was pretty easy is it was done completely through canvas. The textbook is hard to get, try Chegg. She did her best with class due to issues with the hurricane. As a not crim justice major, it was interesting and informative
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: While this class is easy if you do the reading and follow instructions, Charla herself is not always the most helpful. The class is easily manageable without interaction with Charla, though.

This is the easiest and most interesting class I have taken at FSU. I took this for a natural science requirement and was very pleasantly surprised. The course is made up of weekly quizzes (super easy), exams, discussion boards, and a paper. It doesn't take much to pass, and I really enjoyed it!

I love the classes she teaches for CSI however what I do have a problem with is the textbook used. It provides unclear information and even reading the chapters, you don't learn material like one should from a textbook. Minus poor textbook choice, Perdue is great and always available to help.

reviews.comment: I think I can speak for many students when I say that we all pretty much love her, she's great!!

She is a wonderful person.

reviews.comment: The Goat of Teachin has a great music taste
This is the epitome of the professor you dream of having in college.

Great guy to get to know. I had his class for a summer course and he was the very helpful and accessible by email. Just pay attention in class and give moderate effort and you should get an A. Great experience.

reviews.comment: Joe is awesome. I had to take a personal finance class with him for credit. Never in my life have I valued a professor's lectures until I met him! reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Not an interesting course at all. Mind-numbing. Expect to spend hours mindlessly entering numbers into spreadsheets and get points off if you make a slight error. Dr. Calhoun dismissed documentation I sent him after I got hit by a car. Communication with him was so exhausting I haven't bothered disputing my grade. Take Games and Decisions instead.
Super Easy Online Class- However if you miss one assignment it doesn't matter if you're in a coma- he will not let you submit it late or give extra credit.
Super easy, show up and pass

All of my classes have been online since covid hit, and no other prof has made online classes a breeze like this guy. He really gives you so many opportunities to get a good grade by offering optional second life and top hat. He made study guides, lecture videos, practice quizzes, everything you need to succeed!

Three exams on Canvas are fairly easy!

reviews.comment: Lots of outside of class work, you spend at least $100 on outside of class materials. Be careful with the mindtap work its very unclear grading and he is not very understanding when it comes to mindtap. I never went to class and managed to pass, the exam is 43% of your grade too so beware.

Great guy. Fun class. Guidelines are clear from the beginning, and there are lots of grade opportunities. However the final is worth 40% of your grade, of which you need a 95% overall class grade to earn an A. Need to go to class for top hat questions to pass the class. I would take again but spread your studying out for the final so you can pass.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Professor Calhoun is a sweet old man but takes the class as joke until the Final Exam. You HAVE to go to class to pass, and will have a lot of work outside the class. Just because you work hard in this class, DOES NOT mean you will do well. Study hard and do all extra credit cause you will need it for the exam.

reviews.comment: EXCELLENT teacher and a great guy! Very nice dude, very approachable, very funny, great attitude every day in class, no accent, easy to understand, interesting, and STRONG REPUBLICAN! Which in my case was a blessing to see in a classroom, but not so much so that he shoves it down your throat, just enough to point out the blessings of conservatism!

Took micro&macro.He's the best teacher I've had at FSU so far!Definitely recommend him. He's clear about his expectations and the material. Easy to reach.Gives a lot of opportunities to earn grade.Clicker questions are very helpful too and the final review is all the clicker questions from previous classes so print them out!I love him as a teacher!
Great Professor! Very helpful and wants you to do well. The easy assignments will help your grade and he is very straight forward and clear about the expectations of the class.

Amazing guy. Go to class and you will get an A easily. He always shows funny movie clips during class to make it not so stiff. I took him for micro and macro because his classes are never too boring. Take any class from him. He's great!
great guy wll do his best to help you out.  the papers are easy.  make shure you do them on the right book though.  go to class and you'll get an A.
calhoun is a great guy, but doesnt really teach in class. you have to attend every class for prs questions and activity points but does a bad job at actually teaching the class.
Awesome teacher, really nice.  Make sure you do all of the PRS questions because if you do than you don't have to take the exam at the end! Good luck
He is such an odd, but fun teacher
No Comments
No Comments

Professor Weaver grades almost solely based on quiz scores, the notes provided often times do not match the lectures, and I found that many of the questions on his quizzes were completely unrelated to the content discussed in the lecture. I truly would recommend anyone else over him, an absolute joke.

Be prepared to leave his lectures with just as much knowledge you walked in the door with. Reads the notes off of the board, and when a question is asked he will just say "I don't know I didn't write these notes". Unavailable outside of class, if you have the misfortune of having him as your prof... good luck.

Nearly every single quiz given has had bugged Canvas questions. Each quiz has two attempts and showcases the answers meant to be given, and they are still marked as incorrect on a second attempt. When I mentioned this in class, he laughed jokingly about Canvas marking them wrong and doesn't respond to any emails. Avoid this class at all costs.

He is not a good teacher by any means but there are some good things.
1. Only graded on quizzes, no homework. 
2. No final. Only quizzes (which are open notes).
3. If you want to actually understand the content, you won't be able to with his lectures. 
4. He constantly messed up problems and said "I'll have to figure it out later" 

Plan to use gpt

The class struggled with the first exam so she allowed the final to replace the score, which was really great. Overall, the class is not too stressful. Exams are free response so don't skip over the homework, but it mostly comes from the quizzes.

Professor Compton is the ultimate test of one of the most important skills you can obtain in STEM fields: teaching yourself. Oddly, I don't understand a lot of the flack she has gotten: she responds to emails, happily explains questions if you ask them, and gives generous extra credit for tests. The content of the tests is identical to the quizzes.

The class is based on lectures and notes she gets from other teachers that have taught or are teaching the class, but they are fast-paced and hard to follow almost as if they were sped up to 1.50x. The quizzes are doable and tests are based on the quizzes and homeworks, but studying is impossible for this class due to the content being so scrambled

I do not recommend anyone take this class with this teacher online. However, it is doable. The video lectures are pretty awful but the weekly quizzes are usually derived from these videos. The tests are also not unreasonable, and she grades with partial credit. It is a hard class though and if you have questions it is very hard to get answers.
Impossible to deal with, sad teachers like this exist.

I highly do not recommend taking MAD2104 with her. No books were provided, instead it was all pre-recorded videos made by another professor, Dr. Bowers. In which the pre-recorded lectures are unorganized with vague explanations. Also, when you go into MAD3105, you will struggle since 1/3 of the topics were not taught in MAD2104 (I realized that).

Overall the class was not that difficult, however, the structure of the class was awful. Online video lectures are the only way that you are taught and are not even by Compton herself. As for homework, there is a "quiz" every week and the tests for the most part are based on the quizzes. Also, Compton never responds to emails.

Finishing her class for the Summer C session. Definitely do not recommend taking this class. She is almost impossible to contact and what little response she gives usually does not help. She does not actually teach anything and just uses Covid online lectures from other profs. You can also expect to wait multiple weeks for grades on quizzes/exams.

I dont recommend taking her the class is definitely on autopilot so everything you have to figure out on your own. The videos she uses are speed up, like speaking very fast so it's hard to understand. She does not provide any help other then "use what's provide", and it's illegible. Also if you fail one test you'll fail the class, all on honorlock.

Extremely disorganized and unresponsive. Unclear and inconsistent communication, she will often contradict herself. 

The class is passable because the lectures and notes she posts are all made by other professors. It's a mismatched smattering of material and you will have no idea what you will be tested on, but if you study it all you will be fine

Professor Compton was nice, unfortunately the class was on autopilot. We barely had any contact with the professor. I tried to email and call her to setup office hours and received no response. The course didn't have to be so difficult. I spent more time looking for outside resources because the course materials were not effective.

He is a challenging professor but he will prepare you for the CPA exam. He teaches from a powerpoint then gives a quiz for that chapters material. You will probably average a C on the quizzes. But he will prepare you well enough in time for the final exam. The final is no where near as hard as the quizzes! Also hes kinda sassy lol
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: If I were to take audit again, I would not hesitate to take this professor. Do not be turned away by the low reviews. He genuinely offers real-world knowledge as he was recently a senior auditor at KPMG. He actually wants to prepare his students for the CPA, instead of just teaching textbook definitions. Audit can be tricky, but just listen to him.

The material in the class is difficult but fair for an intro audit course however, the quizzes are really hard and sometimes no matter how you study you wont be prepared. Group projects are not explained well but then graded harshly. Final exam was very fair. A good grade is possible, you just have to study. Some of these comments are too harsh.

Everyone giving a rating of a 1 is just angry because they did not apply themselves in this class. I believe this class has served as great preparation for the audit section of the CPA exam. He is extremely knowledgeable in the subject and his lectures are very clear and interesting. Quizzes are difficult and he loves trick questions.

Not having the slides posted before class, confusing quizzes, and harshly graded assignments are the worst parts of the class. The material posted is fair for an intro audit course. One lacking part is not being taught how to do assignments, other than given instructions. It is a really hard class, prof doesn't make it easier. Study a lot for an A!

Extremely condescending and disorganized. He constantly contradicted himself, offered no clear expectations, and gave almost no feedback to help us improve. Quizzes felt impossible even after hours studying. Removing the extra credit at the end of the semester was just another example of him not wanting his students to do well. Avoid.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Ok he definitely wrote the funny thing on his on rmp. The rudest and most condescending professor at FSU. Took away our extra credit at the end of the semester to maintain his "integrity." Speaks down to us and prides himself on knowing more about audit than he does. Guess what? I hope he does that's what Im paying for. Talks down to most of us.

Prof Eisel is a great professor. Yes his quizzes are hard. But he teaches well. There is no reason for him to be rated this low. He has real knowledge in this field and auditing as a career is difficult in itself. He prepares us well, is funny, and a great professor. Anyone who writes a 1 star are just pissed they don't know how to study.

Gave out extra credit opportunity to volunteers in final class, then took it away a week later because "not everyone got the chance to earn it" taking me down a letter grade with nothing I could do. Also told he didn't care about my Doctors note and couldn't make up the quiz I missed. Avoid at all costs, he is contradictory and belittling.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

reviews.comment: There is only 1 cum. final. The final exam study guide he gave is so vague, you basically have to go back into every chapter of the textbook to study. He hardly gave us questions to practice and didn't make any of the in-class quizzes we took this yr. available as a study reference. I've never had a prof. give this LITTLE guidance. Don't take him

I get that his quizzes are tough and he puts in tricky questions but honestly, he teaches well and will admit when he's wrong. This class is really about reading carefully and if you just read the material and put in the work beforehand, you'll be fine. Also, if it's worth anything, he's also a fairly generous grader.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: A 1 is a generous rating for this guy. He has a fetish for seeing his students fail. Quizzes consistently contradict what he teaches in his slides, and he refuses to acknowledge it. In-class assignments are poorly explained and graded extremely harshly. Do not take him.

When we go over quizzes in class everyone cheers when they get an answer correct because we never expect to. He smiles when we miss quiz questions and prides himself on "tricking" his students. Theres ONE cumulative final.I fully expect to just scrape by in this class with a C flat. I made A's in both FARs if that puts it into perspective for you.

Extremely rude professor. Will belittle your thoughts and questions. Read the textbook word for word if you want to pass. He does not usually allow questions. His powerpoints contradict the textbook sometimes so he leaves you guessing with which to use. Quizzes are nitpicky in wording. Good luck.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Professor Gaitros is decent at teaching. The subject isn't easy so you will have to put some effort in learning the material. There is a lot of homework, from written assignments to discussions. Three Honorlock exams, first two are open response and final is multiple choice. Stay on track, put some effort and you will get an A.

Dr. Gaitros is a good professor. The class is pretty straightforward. Couple of writing assignments which are a little time consuming. Some technical assignments which are easy. He is the nicest man in the world!

he's funny, tells you what's on the test, and is entertaining

hard class, hard teacher, very arrogent.. i hate that man

The only thing hard about Farhood's classes is keeping your underwear on during them. The man is smoking hot like I don't even know. Great teacher, learned a lot, did well, but damn. Damn.

Professor Basiri is an excellent teacher, his stories are amazing and very entertaining. Does not follow standard teaching practices and thinks outside the box. Farhood keeps it simple! Gets rid of all apprehensions of what you'd expect of a speech class.Relates and communicates with students well,he will keep u laughing. Sorry the next comment was for 1608 course

the most fun class i have taken yet. hes a young funny guy but also a great professor.

The course material is difficult so don't take that out on him. He is extremely helpful and provides a huge amount of material. I agree with the person that said you need to prioritize what is important and what isn't. That'll take time, but you're given every chance to succeed. He'll challenge you. I can tell Dr. Uh puts a lot of energy into his courses, but he could use some assistance with the online stuff. Dr Uh was super available to help students.

I ended up having to teach this entire class to myself and even then it was hard because his lectures were so confusing and all I've the place that I didn't even know which part of the books to self learn from.

Goldsby is not a good teacher at all. His skill at getting a student that hasnt learned the material before to understand chemistry is very very bad. He makes things overly complicated.
