He is a super nice guy, but lectures can be confusing. Honestly you are going to have to teach yourself most of the material, but that is what college is. This class is not supposed to be easy, so do not be surprise if you receive a low grade if you do not put in effort. He is better than almost any Chem 1 teacher at FSU at the moment.

Goldsby really is not any good at teaching chemistry. While he is good at chemistry, he is lacking in the ability to teach others at it, and you will find that he wastes most of the class period stuttering away precious time

Goldsby seems like a genuinely nice person, and he really tries his best to help us succeed in his class. His lectures were pretty confusing and seemed like they had nothing to really to do with what we were learning. Recitation is what really saved me in this class. I'm sure he'd be more helpful one-on-one.
His lectures do not make any sense. His lectures goes way off topic and his tests are impossible due to the fact that he does not teach the material. Would never take again and do not reccomend.

Dr. Goldsby is definitely a great person, but as a teacher he struggles to provide lectures that are informative and that do not involve going off on some random tangent. CHM 1046 is not an easy class, however his tests are extremely difficult and he does not give any quizzes so your entire grade is based on test performance.

Do yourself a HUGE favor and DO NOT take CHM1046 with him. He's a good guy and all but if you don't have a very, very solid chemistry background, the riddle booklets he calls exams will make your grade drop quicker than the noise level in the class when he cracks a corny joke in lecture.

He is very helpful and really cares about the students. He tries hard to make sure everyone not only understands the material but also understands the inter-workings of why everything works the way it does. He is not a fan of memorization so he will give you all the formulas you may need for the tests. If you try and go to class you will do well

He is easy but at times he tends to go over things very briskly and not go as in depth as the material he puts on the tests. That being said, he is very interesting and the class is very do-able. Can easily get an A if you put effort in studying.
No Comments

He's a really great teacher!! I had him for CHM 1051 and the Independent research lab that went with it.  He's very helpful and really cares about how his students are doing.  I highly recommed him to anyone taking a chemistry class!!!!

Dr. Thomas is genuinely one of the best professors I've ever had. His class is so interesting and you can really tell he's passionate about what he does. He posts the lecture slides on canvas and he doesn't give much homework at all just some reading and an inquisitive on each chapter. He is the best!
LOVE this class and Dr. Thomas. Lectures were so interesting and exams were easy if you went to class. Very light homework too. Highly recommend!
Dr. Thomas clearly loves what he does and it shows!!!! super interesting class but very easy

Amazing class and a very inspired professor who truly seems to enjoy his job. Only assignments were weekly inquizitive homeworks which were basically a guaranteed 100, and the unit tests which were online on canvas from home. Definitely recommend !!

Dr. Thomas was an awesome professor for this class! He made class material fun and easy for non-anthro majors. The class itself was easy, and it is a great option if you need an honors credit. Grading was done very liberally, and we went on two field trips within a 6 week class which was awesome! He is very knowledgable and enthusiastic!

Dr. Thomas was a great professor. Was very knowledgeable about anthropology and made the class fun and interesting between a couple of field trips and in class activities. Class was no too hard, mainly graded on a10 page final paper that was graded pretty liberally. Would definitely recommend taking his honors course.

TAKE THIS CLASS.  It's very fun and you won't regret it.  Prof. Thomas is absolutely amazing! Very funny and laid back and nerdy.  The lectures are so interesting that you will regret having to leave class.

Dr. Thomas is my absolute favorite professor at FSU. He cares a great deal about his students and is completely understanding of his student's needs. I have taken 6 classes with him and plan on taking more. If he is teaching Masculinity from a Global Perspective, take it. It was my favorite class of his! Truly an outstanding professor.

Best professor I've had at FSU. You learn so much and he's passionate about what he teaches which makes lectures and material easy to absorb. Knows a lot about the field he teaches and is always open to talking and working with students who need extra help/want extra experience. 12/10 would recommend.

I took Dr. Thomas for physical anthro as my last science class and he was great. In my opinion he is a real gem at FSU. The material is a little challenging, but he's very easy to approach and his lectures are clear which makes it easier. I'm really glad I took him and definitely would again if I had to.

I had him for Intro to Physical Anthro and would highly recommend this class. Prof. Thomas is hilarious and made class super fun. The whole class was 3 assignments, which weren't terrible, 3 exams, and a final. If you take notes and pay attention to his lectures you will pass with flying colors. Absolutely love him and would take him again.

Kinda funny in a dorky way. Has random attendance days and if you are there, you get one point added to your overall grade, and does this up to 5-7 times. The tests are not jokes and you need to study. He gives so much extra credit, I never once got an A on a test but got an A in the class. Two papers20% of your grade and graded by completion
reviews.rating_tags: GRADED BY FEW THINGS--EXTRA CREDIT--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Amazing class and an amazing professor! He offers random iclicker questions that really boost your grade so there's an incentive to attend class. He lists information to study for each test so it's not hard to do well. It's a great class to sit and really absorb the information, versus scrambling to write down tons of confusing notes.
Dr. Thomas is freaking awesome!

A really great professor that makes class enjoyable. Attendance isn't mandatory, but he does extra credit pop attendance. If you don't like going to class, it would be in your best interest to copy the lecture notes even though the power points are online&he reviews before tests&tells you everything you should go over to be prepared, slide by slide

Great professor,makes the material very interesting, only fault is that the tests are difficult especially if you don't do well on tests or don't retain and memorize well. Would help to have an additional paper to do to help the tests grade and make us do the test in class like a test cuz a lot of us do better on these than tests.
Dr. Thomas is great! He explains all the material very clearly and is helpful and genuinely concerned about his students' sucess. He makes you want to come to class and is very funny too.
Really nice guy. Makes you want to come to class. He cracks jokes all the time that makes the class enjoyable and he's good at teaching the info.
Sweet guy, amazing teacher. If you are looking for a class, take him!
Best professor ever! He's really nice and funny and made class interesting. I would definitely take him again.

Avoid at all costs. Found guilty of sexual misconduct in 2020. There are multiple articles and accounts of him misbehaving as a professor. Just look up John Read Gainsford Sexual Misconduct FSU and it will open your eyes. What a great role model to have.

Wonderful musician and pedagogue. He is so far the best piano professor I had. He doesn't sugar coat anything, very rarely gives (backhanded) compliment and yells at time to time. At the end, he just wants his students to play well.
Prof Gainsford was unbelievable. Not the worst workload but definitely cares about his students. Makes music much more fun and engaging.
No Comments
Great teacher.  changed my life

Dr. Gitter was probably the best teacher I've ever had. He is clear and engaging with his lectures and provides feedback quickly. He offers lots of resources to help you better prepare for exams and has extra credit opportunities. As long as you pay attention and put in effort (study, do ur work, ect), his class is great and you will learn a lot.

Professor Gitter made the material clear and entertaining, which can be difficult on an online course. He explains things deliberately so that the students can understand. Sometimes he did it when I didn't even realize I didn't understand it until he explained it more. An enjoyable class that I had to study for!
He's teaching at Auburn University now, not Florida
No Comments

Bad professor. He single handedly ruined my average. Not only can he not teach, but he goes out of his way to make tests much harder than any homeworks are practices that have been provided. There is no time on the tests and he will add in several more variables on the actually exam. Incredibly frustrating.

PAYING to take a class at FSU meanwhile your "Learning" the material on 3rd party online websites. Bliss is nothing more than a moderator who submits grades off an excel sheet. Not a nice professor. Going through the class to get a passing grade rather than actually learning about finance because of the structure and lack of effort. What a joke
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: If you don't have a religion, pick one and start praying.

Find people to study with. Bliss sucks but his exams are online, no honorlock. They questions are impossible. This class is no cake walk just find friends in the class. Final grade is basically just exams and very little homework.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor is hard to communicate with; notes are old and re-used and assignments are automatically graded. TA's deal with everything. What does the professor actually do? Extremely exam heavy class and all other assignments are basically busy work. In my opinion, he brings unnecessary attitude into the class and holds his ego above all students.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Gary Bliss is the most understanding professor I've ever had. The exams are EXTREMELY hard but VERY manageable if you actually go to office hours or email him. Bought Skoolers and didnt even use it. He provides more than enough resources. Its a weeder class for a reason. FSU asked him to make it harder, not his fault. Incredible guy. Spam homework

This class is the Titanic and Gary Bliss is Rose pushing you off the floating door to your death. Exams are so fast Gary might as well replace dog racing with sleep deprived finance student racing. To get through this class don't use any of his materials, plug in the book material to Chatgpt and ask it to teach it and create practice questions.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Worst professor at FSU, wants everyone to fail his class, avoid him if you can because he does not teach anything that is on the test.

He's a nice man and very knowledgable. However, his tests really are out to get you. I have ran out of time on all of them, he gives you a study outline that is nothing like the tests. He was happy to share that the exam average was a 74 and said that is what he expected. Coming from a straight A student, I made my first C in this class.

Don't believe any reviews before 2024. Exams are on content not in textbook or lectures and some tutoring services are banned. Extremely difficult to pass no matter how hard you study. First exam I've ever failed. If there's another professor available take that class.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: The oldest and worst teacher ever. Spends his life on a boat off in the Atlantic, giving out tests from a test bank thats 50+ years old. It is literally you with 3 months of Finance vs his 80 years of knowledge. He even claims multiple times that "the book answer says I'm wrong, but I don't care cause I think I'm right" in his review sessions.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I haven't even finished the class yet but want to end it all. This class is such a source of anxiety for me and affects my performance in every other class. I understand the concepts and do great in practice, then the exam is 100x harder and things we've never seen. To top it off, Bliss is traveling the world ooo on a boat while I suffer...

honestly, he seems like a nice dude that's been worn out over the years. his classes are overly difficult to the point where you're not even learning, you're just trying to pass. i've heard fin4304's been made a weed-out class and it would add up. not a lot of helpful resources and not enough time to do all questions on exams

A terrible representation of FSU. If this guy gave a talk about FSU, I never would've come. Just a professor whose class you want to avoid at all costs if you can. Hopefully they hire someone else to teach this class
The tests need a massive rework. They're incredibly unclear and impossible to finish on time.

I feel like not enough people talk about this but the time constraints are what really make his exams so hard. One exam I took, I only completed 23 out of 35 of the questions and had to guess on the other 12 because of time. The questions didn't feel terrible but typing all of the data into excel and making all of the calculations felt impossible.

You know there's an issue with the class when you could study all the given lectures and assignments and still not do well on the test. None of that work prepares you for the tests because, for some reason, Gary takes pride in making the exams substantially harder than the material he gives you. Both of his classes are simply ridiculous.

The previous comment states that he curved 2/4 tests which is not true. He gave us some points on one exam because there were issues with the answer key and he emphasized that it wasn't a curve. Then he gave out a couple on the last test to some students because he didn't want to be bothered by people asking for their grade to be rounded.
Skoolers templates will save your life. He also offered EC opportunities and curved 2/4 of the exams. Class takes effort and is not an easy A, but you can tell he cares about his students.

The exams were impossible and did not resemble the homework at all. I've never had an issue with a professor until this class. I put 20+ hours into studying for each of the 4 exams, and every single time there was stuff that wasn't mentioned either in the textbook or the lecture videos.

this was literally the hardest class i have ever taken. there is no point in studying because the exams are 10x harder then the homework and in class examples. he gives some extra credit which helped my grade a lot, but this class was a constant source of anxiety for me. so happy its done.

Only professor that I've had an issue with thus far. He seems nice but just a terrible professor. Review sessions don't help much and you practically have to speed through the tests. He REFUSES to curve tests and just BLATANTLY DISREGARDS how his students perform on them. It seems that many other students feel this way.

Gary Bliss is quite literally the worst professor I have ever had. The exams are legitimately impossible and he does not curve. Even if you spend 20 + hours studying, you will not receive a satisfactory grade. You are better off guessing on every single question. Literally WTF.

This class sucks. Maybe Bliss is a good guy outside of teaching, I have no idea. I think he gets off on making the exams so much harder than the HW. There's some extra credit opportunities, and a ton of HW to offset the exams if you bomb them. Good luck, this is a required course for so many people but it will do literally nothing for you.

Dr. Bliss has no understanding of what test prep is. He consistently provides you with easy homework that is a fraction of the tests' difficulty. He is stubborn and stuck in his ways. If you ask for extra prep materials, he just tells you to review the normal stuff that is already pointless. You can study over and still will not be prepared.

Class seems pretty straight forward until you get to the exams that are nothing like what he teaches. It's 10x harder than any assignments and it's like being introduced to new material during exams. He says time is our biggest enemy for exams, but it's crazy that we are tested on speed rather than knowledge. Seems like good guy but def a bad prof

This class gave me cancer. On the exam, you have to manually make a sheet in excel full of numbers and calculate multiple ratios and equations with those ratios, and do all of this 30 times in under 90 minutes. Would say stay away but this class is mandatory and he's the only one who teaches it, so all I can say is good luck.

I've had teachers that were arrested for assault on students in the classroom that were better teachers than bliss. He is so rude and full of himself and thinks hes better than you even though hes on a teachers salary. He loves making students lives harder and takes pride in being a rude and mean teacher. Dont take this class.

It is very troubling that I feel unmotivated as a student in this class.I have no desire to even study because I feel like it won't even make a difference on the exams. Was hoping for a 4.0 this semester but with this professor I can kiss that goodbye. He is the first professor I've had an issue with here.

The class is really just discouraging because you can put in the time to study, just for the test to be completely different than the assignments. It is just really disappointing; not much else to say. Unfortunate that I have to leave a negative review here, but if you feel the same way then leave a comment on here or in the class evaluations.

He just does not seem to care about the performance of his students on his tests which is cause for concern. I also feel like the feedback provided by students is not being considered whatsoever. The same issues seem to be reoccurring: the tests are overly difficult for no reason and they do not resemble the homework or even the lectures.
What a rude guy. So full of himself. I don't think you can avoid taking this guy unfortunately. Just do what you're told and don't expect him to be nice in any way.

Worst professor at FSU. How can you teach the same class for decades and still suck. Tests are more difficult than they should be. Online lectures are difficult to understand at times. Like he'll spend 5 mins repeating the same rudimentary topic and then gloss over the complex little nuances he'll put on the test. Don't take him if u can help it!!!

Gary needs to retire, he does not teach you anything. Lecture videos are a waste of time and don't be fooled by the open note exams. Test questions are very difficult and are often not even covered in class.

Please don't take this decrepit dust bag. He does not put his students first and has made his tests impossible and hates giving any extra credit. Also, if you have accommodations he just wont curve your test so watch out. Honestly the class is fine but he makes it impossible to get an A, but not because the material is advanced, its because of him.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: This is by far the worst online class I've taken at FSU. Unfortunately, there are no other options for Business majors and you are going to have to take him. There is homework every week, but the real problem are the tests which are SUPER HARD and nothing like the HW. Don't take if you don't have to.

His TAs are a menace to society and make the test way harder than it needs to be. Dr. Bliss is not good at teaching the concepts and most of the course will be self-taught. He doesn't use a mic in a lecture hall so It is hard to hear with all the squeaking chairs from the 90s. Overall he is a great person and is very helpful with you call him or go

Gary bliss is one of the longest running teachers at FSU, which makes it so confusing as to why he is still so bad at teaching this class. Exams are much more difficult than any practice given. He explains the formula but uses many finance terms nobody knows, and no reasoning.Overall, expect to need a tutor if you need this for your future career.

Wouldn't write this if I didn't think this guy was absolutely deserving of an awful review. In fact, this is the first professor I have reviewed on here. I actually created this account to warn others of how bad of a professor he is. I think my biggest problem with him is that the tests are nothing like the homeworks. Would NEVER take him again.

If this class is not a requirement for you, by all that is holy do NOT take this class with this professor. This is the first grade I will receive that is not an A. The exams are nothing compared to the homework problems, and expects you to apply concepts that aren't taught.... Did I mention that you should not take this class by any means.

Wouldn't write this if I didn't think this guy was absolutely deserving of an awful review. In fact, this is the first professor I have reviewed on here. I actually created this account to warn others of how bad of a professor he is. I think my biggest problem with him is that the tests are nothing like the homeworks. Would NEVER take him again.
I'm convinced any positive reviews here were written by him.
Avoid at all costs. Might as well not even watch the lectures because you'll just wind up teaching yourself all the information. Tests are very hard for no reason.

Words cannot describe how ATROCIOUS this professor is. Let me begin by saying the tests are substantially more difficult than the homework and they are much different than the given assignments. This professor goes out of his way to make things more difficult for you. How he is a professor is beyond me because his teaching abilities are horrendous.
The class is pretty tough, but if you put in the work and use Unstuck study for study prep, you can do well. Professor Bliss is hit-or-miss - go to office hours for extra help.

I get that the class is difficult, but he makes the exams nearly impossible. I put a ton of work into the class and can't get a better grade then people who use straight gpt because its so difficult. Seems like he tries to make every exam "non-cheatable" but just makes it almost impossible for people to do it without cheating.

Dr. Bliss was one of the most caring profs at FSU. I highly encourage to go to his office hours, he is so helpful when it comes to homework/test prep. Also, make sure to practice/spend time on homework because he can see how much time was spent so if you ever need to discuss a grade the first thing he will do is see how much work you're putting in.
Sending my future child to UF after taking this class.
Awful teacher tests are half his hw questions half material he doesn't teach you rude towards students overall just the worst class/teacher i think ive ever taken

Honestly, this was by far one of the worst teachers i have ever had to take. He gives lots of information to prepare for tests that are NOTHING like the info he would teach us. Some of the questions on his exams were lined up but most are made up by him. Does not care what so ever towards his students, and refuses to help your grade. Horrible
if u know how to study ur chillin. class not hard all these ppl just started studying the day before the exam.

IMO much easier than the FIN class but still somewhat difficult. Textbook is pretty much useless and his lecture vids aren't too helpful when it comes to understanding the HW (which will take you a bit to do). He comes in clutch with vids on solving nearly every HW problem tho.

Very rude towards his students who ask for extra help. Study for a week straight because the homework is nothing like his exams. He allows 2 flashcards for the exams but don't you dare ask him for help because he will rudely shut you down and make sure to not curve your grade.

what a di**! do not take him if you can take another professor. he goes out of his way to be extremely rude when you ask for extra credit and makes it known that he will single you out from 800 students and make sure to not give you a curve, while giving everyone else one. exams are difficult, he allows 2 flashcards of material but is not helpful.

I'm taking him for both classes and it is clear he hardly knows his own material. He repeatedly during his live review sessions gets questions on his own review wrong and chastises students for asking elaboration on questions. Only Professor in my 3 years at FSU that I can say with confidence is a bad teacher and you need to avoid at all costs.
I disliked the professor.

The best professor I have had so far. Negative reviews are not regarding the professor but rather the material. He teaches a difficult class therefore the work will be difficult. Nonetheless, he is a very charismatic person willing to answer any questions you may have and takes great value and importance in building relations with his students.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This class is composed of 64% of the grade from four tests and 36% from homework assignments. The lectures were alright, but the homework takes several hours to complete. The tests are harder than he lets on, so be prepared. He puts stuff on the tests never discussed in the lectures. Gary and this class are okay, but it should be redesigned.

The hw is super easy, tests are super hard. Get schoolers if u want any hope of understanding, as there's not much instruction. always available for help, but just a hard class. 4 tests, and one hw a week. wouldn't take again.
Other reviews are simply students who don't put in the work. Lectures are concise easy to understand, concepts are well explained, homework problems basically mimic the test.
I learned everything from Skoolers and nothing from professor bliss.

Class is interesting but very challenging. The lecture videos are very barebones and either gloss over or completely omit material that is present on exams (70% of grade). Previous classes were allowed a notecard but they were removed to prevent "cheating", and it felt as we were getting punished for a  previous student's actions.

We didn't get a note card and have to memorize way too many formulas, which just seems unreasonable. Skoolers is a must. Unless, of course, you decide to trick honorlock and have someone else take your test for you, which sadly a lot of people do. The fact that bliss looks the other way makes him just embarassing to the university.

It's clear previous classes got lucky, as a note card used to be allowed on the exams. It still says it is in the syllabus leaving many students confused right before the first exam. Now you are stuck trying to remember how to do hundreds of problems off the top of your head. Says things won't be on the exam yet appear anyway. Cannot recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Lectures are all right, seems I be a bit of a whiny guy, removing things such as study guides and notecards on a whim. Was disappointed to be honest because he seemed nice in his recorded lectures.

Professor Bliss has good intentions. He is a funny guy and tells a bunch of stories in his lectures. He is knowledgable about what he is talking about but the lectures are somewhat a waste of time. He goes over HW questions in lecture which is somewhat helpful but I wish he spent more time going over new material. He often rushed through it.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Amazing professor. Always ready to help and answer all your questions IF YOU'VE DONE YOUR WORK. Fundamental coursework doesn't put much pressure on you.

This online class consisted of 60 minutes of lectures and a few homework help videos. The homework help videos are great for helping you understand the homework. Unfortunately, the tests consist of math problems not found on the homework and full of concept questions that he never even mentions. I took the 6 week class so maybe 16-week is fine.

Dr. Bliss is one of the professors that truly wants you to do well, but you have to show up to office hours and get to know him if finance doesn't come naturally to you. Attending class isn't very helpful but his office hours are a game-changer.
Top G

I had Dr. Bliss for Finance and QMB. He is my favorite professor on this campus. Utilize his office hours. He talks very quickly in lectures and it is easy to fall behind if you don't rewatch his lectures or go in for help. He genuinely wants all of his students to succeed.

I was scared to take this class because I heard it was really hard, but it really was not as bad as I thought. You do have to put effort into the class if you want to do well on the tests, but I found it to be manageable. He gave lots of extra credit this semester. I found in person lectures to be useless so I just watched the online ones.

I've taken him once before for QMB, and I wasn't excited to see that he was the only professor available for this class. I'm not a finance major, and this course with this guy teaching seemed impossible for me. His video lectures absolutely suck and don't help at all. If you're not a finance person, try your best to steer clear from this guy.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: 1) you better buy skoolers, 2) The grade you have after the final exam isn't your final grade, 3) most boring lectures right before acg2021 and 2071, 4) do not take this class or teacher. I studied for days on days to get an A on the final exam. Got me up to a B- was so excited. Now I have a 79. Weeks after final exam my grade changed. NOT FAIR.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Honestly, after reading these reviews I was hesitant to take this class but it wasn't as bad as everyone says. Yes, this class is hard, no doubt about it but you can definitely still get a solid B as long as you understand the Excel questions (exactly like the hw). Memorize them!! Conceptual questions are hard so def study those until you get them

I got an A in the class. The Fall 2021 semester was "re-worked" so I didn't know what to expect. People who complain are the lazy ones. I went to his in person office hours and he was happy to help. Don't spam him with useless emails. Skoolers helps but not needed if you thoroughly understand the HW problems.
Horrible professor, does not care about students. Also makes the class 10000 times harder than it should be

Even with skoolers, this class is difficult. The course was apparently rewritten this year and is way harder than it needs to be for an introductory finance class. The content seems straightforward enough, but the exams have a way of making it confusing. Would absolutely advise against taking this class if you can avoid it.

Dr. Bliss seems like a good guy and teacher. The class however is awful. I compare it with ACG2021 and ACG2071, if you have taken those. If I had known there was a skoolers I would have 1000% done that. If you like extreme memorization and impossible concept questions or if you want to study for weeks just to fail the exam then this class is for u.

Professor Bliss can be a nice guy, but I recommend finding a different professor for FIN3403 or QMB3200. I took both with him, and I relied on Skoolers in order to pass. I am sure you have heard before that some people can be very knowledgeable on a topic, but have no idea how to teach it to others. Bliss fits into that category.

his lectures suck (he barely explains the actual content), go straight to the homework videos and you will be ok. his exams are based on mainly homework so make sure you know how to do them. skip studying the concept questions and just write them down on the index card. I didn't really like this class or him (he's very rude) - if you can avoid, do.
Class is not bad but do yourself a favor and get skoolers

Dr Bliss was a great and respectful professor. He truly enjoys what he does and it shows in his teaching. I bought Skoolers for the course and never watched a single one of his videos and got a 95 in the course. His homework is what you need to know to succeed on the exams and also do his study guides, they mirror exactly what the exam will be like

take this IN-PERSON (especially if you have a hard time setting few hours to watch lectures). not even skoolers will guarantee an A in this class (the fact that many people have to get skoolers in the 1st place is stupid since we are already paying for the class but oh well that says a lot about the prof & his teaching). TA is nicer to reach out to

His mic sucks, no extra credit offered, no curve either. He ignored people's calls and emails when canvas was down (the second time), and didn't care that some people got to do 2 exams without honorlock. He doesn't care about students and is very rude. I gave up on taking QMB3200 because I don't wanna take him again.
Just do everything! I got Skoolers and didn't watch a single one of his videos and got an A. Put in the effort to learn the material and you'll be great!
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Dr. Bliss is a great guy. I took FIN3403 and QMB3200. His lectures are easy to understand and he really does try, but I feel like the only reason people do so well is because they shell out the money for skoolers. Make sure youre proficient in excel and youll at least pass, but if you want a higher grade its going to cost you.

Dr. Bliss is an AMAZING professor. He is very fair and grades based on homework and exam scores. He provides videos to help you through homework problems. Make sure you know how to do the homework problems easily and you'll get an A on the exams! I got skoolers because I thought I would need it, but you can get an A without it.

Really not difficult if you put any effort in. He's a good teacher and a good person overall whos understanding. I put no effort in all semester had a C+ and got a 94 on the final when I studied. The quizzes are useful to study, so reviewing those is key. He curved our entire class up like a whole percent after we already got 2% extra.

Stats can be tricky but Dr. Bliss makes his tests very fair. The HW assignments take a long time, but once you figure out how to do all the problems, they are a great study tool, as his test questions are exactly like his hw problems. He also allows you to use a cheat sheet notecard on each exam. This class can be completed without Skoolers.

Bliss is a good professor. Lectures are broken down well so they aren't overly long. However, if you do not keep up with them, they will take hours to watch. He does a good job of explaining things. If you have questions or concerns, he is readily available almost all the time. You can tell he really loves the class and wants everyone to succeed.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Better hope he doesnt forget the extra credit so you dont have to email him an hour before grades are due. He said he hated doing extra credit because it was so hard for manually enter two points into canvas. That tells you everything you need to know about this professor.
Good Professor. You need to get Skoolers for this course. It will really help you do well for the exams.

I would highly recommend buying skoolers for the course. You may think you wont need it at first but youll definitely appreciate it in the end. Given that this semester was fully remote, Bliss would always mention how difficult its been for everyone, yet he always hated extra credit being brought up.

Great class that is not hard if you study and know how to do all HW questions and study guide questions. Normally doesn' give extra credit but gave this semester chance of to 3% added to overall final grade. I didn't watch any lectures, just get skoolers and practice and the tests really are not that difficult. Test 1 easy, 2 is hardest.3&4 ight

Seems like a nice man, but taking his class online has been a nightmare. His lectures are confusing and often double back on each other, making small variations in content very hard to understand. Long boring lectures followed by long boring homework (at least he usually gives you most of the homework answers). The exams cover too much material.

Take him!! one of the best business professors out there. I had him during COVID fall and he was so understanding and willing to help with anything, he even gives us his personal cell number and encourages us to call him when we need help. This class is NO walk in the park but with his help and Schoolers packets, you'll do just fine.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: A terrible professor. He has absolutely no regard for his students or for their lives outside of class. He is extremely unsympathetic and frankly, just mean. 10/10 do NOT recommend this professor if you intend to learn something or pass.

Great teacher- will help with anything if you just reach out to him and he makes himself so available. If you get skoolers or just pay attention to his lectures and exam review you will do well- just don't fall behind or procrastinate. It's a lot of work but all super doable.

The material is dense but you get a notecard for each exam and a really detailed study guide/breakdown of the questions. He breaks information down well and is happy to help students outside of class. There's no reason you shouldn't do well.

I am taking him for both QMB and FIN and he's great. They are tough classes but he lays out the online content in a very organized way that you know what to do and when. If you put in the work, there isn't much to worry about. He also makes his phone number available for students during covid, which is pretty nice. Great guy.

Both classes I've taken with Dr. Bliss have been extremely frustrating. While his videos have improved slightly, he still wastes time on topics that he doesn't test on. Further, he teaches oversimplified examples in lectures; homework and exam problems are much more difficult and complex than what is taught. My TA essentially taught me the course.

Just finished the summer semeser and WOW it was fast. Six weeks with 12 HW's, 4 quizzes, and a cumulative final made this class my full time job lol. Bliss is a great professor tho and very accessible. GET THE SKOOLERS REVIEWS THEY WILL BE A LIFE SAVER!

Bliss is a great Prof, but taking FIN3403 over the Summer Semester (6 weeks) is a PAIN. If you can, take in the fall/spring. The class is not really hard, but you have an exam every week. The course is not condensed during the summer semester. ALSO be ready for a cumulative final. 12+ Chapter Quizzes and Five Exams over a six week period. STUDY!

So much material. Weekly homework and 4 open note quizzes with a closed note final .. definetly a GPA killer. I took it in 6 weeks and i'm not sure if getting over with it quickly was worth the stress.

Bliss is by far the best professor I have ever encountered hands down His classes are hard math classes so the material is difficult But Bliss explains the material in such an easy to understand and relevant way He VERY adequately prepares you for exams and does so much to help students who actually work An inspiration please take him

This Professor does not want you to learn, at least in the online section. Online lectures are extremely vague, and I had to teach it all to myself using Chegg. Plus what's the point of 4 open note quizzes with a closed-book final, I have no idea how to do anything. Ugh.

Fantastic professor. The classes( FIN3403 and QMB3200) are both difficult math classes so a challenge is expected. He presents hard information in a very easy to understand way. Make sure you understand the homework problems VERY WELL and you will do fine on the exams they are very similar but require a little more critical thinking.

When studying for this class, make sure to redo the homeworks and the study guide until you are confident in the material. If you're confused with the material go see a TA! I couldn't get through this class without Andrew! Also, watch the webinars Bliss has before each exam. You have to work for your grade. No extra credit.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Overall, easy class if you study for the exams. Study the hw problems, and DO the study guide questions. Skoolers will definitely help as well with the exams. I thought it was easier than QMB. Exams are fairly straight forward and similar to hw.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: QMB isn't hard with Bliss as long as you watch his videos and understand how to do the homework. His exams (there are 3) are very very similar to the homeworks. You are allowed a 3x5 notecard so that's helpful. I don't suggest waiting until the last day(s) to study... definitely keep up with the work and you'll do fine.

Dr. Bliss is a rockstar and one of the most caring professors in the COB. The class is not overly difficult and if you put in the effort, you can and will make an A. The concepts aren't hard and the resources provided will make everything simpler, and easier come exam time. Minimal homework and a notecard allowed on the exam. Bliss is great!

Dr. Bliss is a nice guy who wants you to well. FInance can be tough so you have to put in a lot of time and effort. Watch his lecture videos and take notes before doing the homework, which takes a very long time, but help you learn the material. The tests are just like the HW and study guide questions, and you're allowed a notecard cheat sheet.

I was expecting this class to be my hardest this semester but it was my easiest. You can take the homework quizzes as many times as you want so those are easy points. His tests are the study guide questions with different numbers and the study guide questions are all from the homework. He creates videos throughly explaining how to do the homework.

His exam reviews and homework practices are all you need. Everything else is useless. He is so rude and disrespectful, that "nice guy" facade should be taken with a grain of salt. As soon as things don't go his way, he starts to get inconsiderate and snappy. He was deadset on proving that he had control over things that he didn't.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Very respectful to the students. He makes this class as easy as he could, even though this is supposed to be a hard class. The material isn't too bad, and he provides many videos to watch. He gives students his personal phone number to contact him anytime they need help. This class is very easy (homework/quizlet), just take 3-5 days/exam to study.

At least for the online version of his class, wouldn't even consider him a teacher. Skims through tough topics without even really addressing how to solve tough problems. Be prepared to buy skoolers. They explain the material so much better. Nice guy but one of my least favorite online classes.

I did well in this class because of Skoolers. He's a nice guy but his lectures are hard to follow sometimes. He also uses MyLab which is absolutely terrible. Homework assignments take forever and they are weekly. The exams are fair if you have the Skoolers notecard.

knew this class would be tough going into it bc of all the warnings i'd heard. only 3 exams. the first wasn't too bad but a bit of a wake-up call. highly suggest getting skoolers chapter reviews bc it is the ONLY way to understand how to complete the hw in its entirety. exam reviews/note card for exam are a life saver. 2nd and 3rd exam aren't easy

What should be a simple class seems somewhat impossible. I don't know if anyone else has tried to use the textbook for clarification, but it is the most useless piece of literature to ever exist. The amount of homework we have is soul-crushing. It can take HOURS to finish assignments. SKoolers helped, maybe if it wasn't online it wouldn' be so bad.

Really nice guy, but let me be honest. His online and in person classes are both unorganized. His homework, videos (which were recorded years ago and are no longer applicable to the current course), test, and study guides do no relate to each other AT ALL. Its as if you're taking four separate courses at once. I learned nothing from him. SAD!

Finance is hard and he tries to make it understandable. If I am honest, he did not really teach this course well from an online standpoint. Going to skoolers was the only way I could learn and I would 100% recommend it because they make the index cards that you can bring to the test and if you use that you are guaranteed to pass. Its hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Finance is just a hard class to begin with. Professor Bliss wants students to do well so he gives lots of practice materials for exams, which are all multiple choice. You need to put effort into the class to do well. There are weekly homework assignments and 4 exams throughout the year. I used Skoolers for test reviews and did alright.

Professor Bliss is my favorite professor at FSU. His classes are not easy but he is extremely willing to work with you. In office hours he will go over anything and everything you need help with. He definitely shows he cares that his students actually learn the material. Also took him for QMB 3200 and it was the same level of difficulty.

Took him over Summer B and I would not recommend unless you have to take it over Summer. Bliss is a nice professor and my TA always answered my emails quickly. However, I think this class might be too much for most people in 6 weeks. Its a LOT. I was constantly doing homework on MyLab. His lectures were really fast and hard to follow.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Bliss does a very good job of presenting difficult information in an easy to understand way and offers many opportunities for questions to be answered. Attendance is not mandatory, but if you skip class a lot like I did you will pay for it. Tests are hard, but reasonable since this class is supposed to be tough.
You've got to do the homework and know the review to pass the test with an A or B. Dr. Bliss is a welcome change from the previous professor. I will take another class taught by him.
Professor Bliss is so nice and helpful. The exams were easy if you actually studied and put in effort. So glad I got to take this hard class with him.

Changed the entire grading scale during Finals Week! and expected us to refrain from even contacting him about it. It's his first year teaching this class and it shows, his lectures are long and inarticulate. Be prepared to spend 3+ hours on each of his 30 homework assignments that cost you $100 to even access.
Gary is so nice! He helps you with everything!
Way to much HW dude chill!! Im doing like 12 hrs worth of HW weekly!! Even Spring Break!

Honestly, this class is really easy (gets easier every exam) and Bliss is probably the most accessible teacher at Florida State. If you are in the online class, drop by in person for advantages and updates he fails to give to the online version. You get a note card for every exam so doing poorly is solely on you. Do the homework and master excel.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Gary is a very approachable and nice guy. His first exam was the hardest but the second and last were really easy. I do feel like if I didn't get skoolers then I wouldn't have gotten the same grade. For exam 1, a lot of the content on the exam wasn't covered in class or the homework but overall the class was really easy.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: The material in this class can be confusing if you don't have a solid understanding of excel or statistics, but it really isn't that bad. Watch the lectures, and do the homework and you will be fine. Bliss is incredibly willing to help if you have any questions and even gives you his personal phone number.

online can drop into his in person class. i took in person to save $ but barely go. he has module videos online that are important and helpful online office hours once a week. he is one of the few that really cares about his students. genuine guy. you get a notecard for exams. 3 exams total and then homework is the grade break down.

He's well-intentioned. But he goes off on a tangent during our 50 min class so much that there's not much time to discuss the actual material. Ur grade is weighted 20% hw & 80% exams, 100% NO extra credit. My advice is, go to the office hours. Ask me how I know more about the state of his boat during the hurricane than statistics.

The tests are not like the lectures, just like the homework is neither like the lecture videos or tests. If you do not pay the money for Schooler's, you will not do well. The lack of consistency in the way the material is presented, taught, and tested, really leaves a lot to be desired.

Bliss is the man, hands down but don't take that to mean he isn't paying attention. He is. He knows when we are watching class or not, so make sure to watch it (I had it on in background a lot). Go to skoolers, even Bliss says you should (the free market, he says!). Tests can be gnarly but he makes it make sense. Would 100% recommend.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Participation matters--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: I think he means well and is very accessible but he gets so off topic in his lectures that it makes it hard to stay focused. If you can, take this class during summer B because your homework and quizzes are worth more, and your final exam is worth 30%.

Doing the homework before the last second is super critical in my point of view. Exam's can be tricky however if you study and use the index card to your advantage (meaning actually be able to read it) you should have plenty of time during the test. Dr. Bliss is super willing & eager to help if you ask him for it. If your not a fan of Excel start!!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: If you don't wait until the last minute to do the homework you'll be fine. The exams are similar to the homeworks.
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Gary is so nice and genuinely cares about his students. Super accessible outside of class and always willing to answer questions. The class is graded by 3 exams and homework (15% I think). Doing the weekly homework helps you learn the material. Overall the class is like a combo of Excel and Stats. And you get a notecard for the exams which is perf.

Dr. Bliss was hands down the most helpful professor I have had at Florida State so far. He responded to emails very quickly and truly cares about your success. However, this class was not an easy A. I probably spent a total of ten hours studying before each exam. Homework was not too bad, usually took me an hour or two to finish.

I think Bliss catches heat on here because he teaches a very difficult weed-out class for many business majors. With that being said, Bliss is the most available prof I've ever had. Always responds to emails and even gives you his personal cell number to call with questions. Posts lectures online. Use skoolers tutoring!
reviews.rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Professor Bliss is really a caring guy. Go to his office hours and you'll see what I mean. He helped me through the last section of content, which was really tough and even recommended some tutors at skoolers to help. Overall, I would take him again if he taught any of my future classes.

Easily the worst teacher I have every had at FSU. He cares about the subject but is not a good teacher. He goes around the answers and got homework problems wrong MULTIPLE times throughout lectures. Homework is somewhat helpful and is the only way I learned from this class. The tests are harder than they should be and are the majority of your grade

Dr Bliss really is a bad instructor. He does not give clear instructions about the homework and papers. He is constantly complaining about the students and gives out zero very easily. He accused me of plagiarism more than two times and deliberately gave me zeros just because he thought I was not able to compose such essay. He does not care about us

Dr. Bliss is a very hard professor. He don't provide a clear understanding of the work, and expect you to know what you are doing in the class without his help. When you ask him to explain something about the assignment, he go around the question leaving you still confuses. I don't recommend you take him online.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Bliss is ok for the most part. He definitely loves to go off topic, but he will go back and talk about the subject at hand and has sort of clear expectations on what to expect for the exams. The second exam was really hard, but the first and final exam were manageable, although there's a lot of material on the exams in general.

This class made me hate fall 2017 so much. His lectures are unnecessarily long, he takes hours before actually showing you how to do the problems on excel (tests are done on excel). He literally makes so many mistakes in his lectures, it's sad. you have 2 hours per test but will not have enough time to finish all the questions. Glad i passed
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Tests are tough--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: I would be shocked if he could get a 100% on his own exams. The lecture videos are very long and boring, they could be cut in half if he didn't ramble on about nonsense. The class should be an easy A but he makes it way harder than it needs to be. The homework and exams are nothing alike. Overall, this class and teacher were awful. Would not take

Does not clearly communicate information, goes off on tangents, and makes the class more difficult than it needs to be. Tests are often too long for the amount of time given. Put in A LOT of study time and you can pass

He's a tough guy, don't get me wrong. Southern sense of humor, but also holds southern values. SHOW him you're trying. There were multiple weekly webinars in my online class of 500+ students, yet only the same 4-7 people showed up. Was borderline C+/B- in his class after final, but because I made an effort, I was given a Solid B. TRY
Hard class, go to Skoolers Tutoring to pass

He regraded a month old quiz during FINALS WEEK and took up to 12 pts BACK from students. He made a mistake and noticed it a month later during finals week and decided to change it regardless. He speeds through the rather bland lectures and assumes everything is crystal clear. Doesn't explain quiz questions afterwards. Gotta contact him for help.

Didnt use book alot, note card is life on tests, study lectures and practice test yourself a lot. He jumps through material in online video lectures as if you completely grasped the lessons before it. study study, QMB class isnt easy.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: do not buy book. waste of $, havent opened it once. class is way harder than it needs to be but it is doable. tests can be difficult & his lectures arent the best. pretty average college class if you ask me.

Dr. Bliss operates under the assumption that his class is easy and anyone who doesn't understand just isn't trying hard enough. Overall very condescending, while seems to be a genuine/nice guy outside of class, just did not enjoy him as a teacher. Would not take again.

At first Mr. Bliss class was one of the hardest classes I took in my MBA class.  When I heard I had to take him again I demanded my success coach find me someone else, but after I learned his grading style passing his course was a breeze.  I am happy to have him in my next course.
This professor is condescending and facetious.  He is different with online adults versus traditional students.  He has absolutely no respect.  This may be taken down like the other negative posts.

Dr. Bliss is a great teacher! He is super fun and outgoing. This Finance class is not too hard. Just show up and listen and you will pass. I took it in the summer so it was shorter and we skipped a lot of uninteresting material. He is very nice and respectable.

By the end of the semester we all wanted the class to be over with. What made the class bearable was Dr. Bliss' help with the subject. If anyone complains about Bliss is because they did not study and they did not ask for help. I would not have got good grades without going and asking questions and studying.VM
AWESOME TEACHER

Great professor. Extremely experienced in the financial world and has extensive knowledge on the subject. Teaches the class and brings in real world examples. The class vibe is always great and he encourages class feedback and participation. Not only did I thoroughly enjoy class but Dr. Bliss is an honestly great person.

He will do all he can to help you understand finances, and he's a GREAT teacher!  I had him as a summer instructor at FSU-PC and learned---really learned---the financial markets (a subject I knew nothing about beofre).  If you reaaly want to LEARN, take courses with him!!!

If I weren't so close to finishing, I would transfer because the business department used to be the best one on campus and now it is the worst one.  He is unprepared in class and has ruined the program. If you can--transfer or don't start here!!

Finance is a tough subject, so be prepared to study.  Bliss knows his stuff and is extremely helpful in and out of class. He can go off on tangents sometimes, but for the most part he is great. His in class examples and homework assignments really help drive processes and helped burn the information into my brain.  Highly recommend Professor Bliss!
He is the best teacher I have ever had. He is very helpful and such a fun professor. He is always happy.  Love him!! Alicia
You have one choice with him and that is to study, if you don't study you will fail. Remember this, GO TALK TO HIM, it will help you out in the end
Outstanding professor.  Very intelligent, enthusiastic and current.

I had Gary as a professor in 1992 (?!) Day 1: He teased me about using liquid paper to correct my notes. (FTR I rarely took notes). Day 2: I "responded" by writing "Ignorance is Bliss" on the chalkboard before he arrived, which he found quite amusing. I interned for TSB and built a successful career as an Advisor with a major B/D. Thanks Gary!

I had Mr. Bliss's class in the Spring of 1985.  He was an exceptional instructor. One who had real life practical experince that enabled him to teach the theory and the real life side of business and finance.  Twenty plus years later, I still recall some of our discussions.  I have no doubt that much of my success is due to him.
GREAT TEACHER!  WILL HELP IN ANY WAY HE CAN!!!!
Great Teacher
Awesome professor, really knows his stuff.

Mr. Bliss, and this class, are not as bad as people make them out to be.  The subject is finance, so of course it's pretty difficult; However, he is very willing to help you if you need.  You must do his study guides and understand them, as they are very similar to the questions on the test.  It is a tough class, though, so study hard!

Mr. Bliss' Finance classes were very difficult, yet he was more than willing to go the extra mile to help someone in need.  I learned more from his classes than I did in any other while at FSU-PC.  I constantly find myself referencing the information I learned in his class in my professional career.  I highly recommend this professor!

Great guy! I barely made it thru the first two classes with him and was feeling lost. He was encouraging in person and by e-mail. There are no power points. Get good partners for the project and papers! Communicate with Bliss, ask all the questions you have and he will help! Also ask about the boat, son, and lost fingers, he's good for stories....

Prof. Bliss tends to talk about himself more than the material. Study powerpoints that he gives and really know the material. He used to be a president at a bank, so he expects u to understand at the same level.  I didn't buy a book, but I would suggest it. Don't miss any day of class or else he deducts 8% of your grade each time.
No Comments
Great guy!
Great professor who cares about her students and will always work with you. Truly cares and wants you to succeed in the class.

Professor Venturini was honestly amazing. Her lectures are straightforward and she explains everything very clearly. A whole semester after taking this class I still remember a lot of what we learned. The exams are easy as long as you study. I would recommend going to class. She was very accommodating. Great prof.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Tests are 75% of your grade but there is extra credit for the weekly quizzes. She is a very caring & relatable teacher. I found her class quite difficult and you have to study and read a lot. I have an A but that might change as the semester ends. She is a very nice teacher just a challenging class. You could get an A-C if you put in the work!

Very nice professor. The course overall was very easy. Wad only graded by tests & quizzes but all were fairly easy. Reading for the class consisted of a lot of articles. If i wasn't literally dumb I would have gotten an A.
Only graded off of exams and quizzes but they were pretty easy. A lot of article readings.
Dr. Hadi is the best professor in the EE undergraduate course work. If you can take all of his classes

Dr. Hadi is an excellent instructor whose other classes I will definitely be taking as I progress. His lectures are excellent and he goes through tons of example problems allowing the student to understand the application and practice in class. He also is very available outside class for extra help and is very kind, patient, and caring.

He is definitely one of my favorite professors! I like that he inserts his own explanations on everything, not just going by the book. It helps a lot. He also doesn't mind spending extra time with students, he wants to make sure we understand. Very helpful, very respectful, and polite.
Challenging material. Dr. Hadi is excellent and explains thoroughly. However, tests are a challenge
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Inspirational--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: This is the most advanced course currently taught by Dr. Hadi -- Despite the complexity of the work, the material is always presented clearly and with application in mind. The tests are hard, but the focus is less so on the tests and more so on the application. Dr. Hadi is ALWAYS willing to help and explore concepts further.

Dr. Hadi is exceptional at conveying challenging concepts in a fun and interesting way. While course material is covered somewhat quickly in Circuits 1, any time I had any questions or issues understanding the material, I could pop into his office hours or shoot him an email and he would always be there to help. Overall a very enjoyable experience.

Dr. Hadi demonstrates the highest level of respect for his students -- This class is by no means easy but is made much more digestible by the great lectures and notes and ties in extremely well with the lab -- Be prepared to work hard and as it will be well rewarded

The class and lab go together well. Everything taught in lecture will be used in the lab. So, just going to lectures and doing the labs will help prepare students for the exams. However, there are only 3 of them and they are multiple choice. So, I recommend doing the few homework problems that he recommends, although you don't turn it in.

Dr. Hadi's a great teacher whose lectures are well prepared and demonstrate an expertise in the course material. His lectures move fast, carry a lot of content, and provide several examples related to the core concepts. This course requires a lot of studying for one to be successful, and he does provide helpful feedback outside of the classroom.

Dr. Hadi is a brilliant professor and gives engaging lectures. However, he moves fast and the material is challenging. In order to do well in the class, it will take study time and work. However, Dr. Hadi is more than willing to help. Plus, all lectures are recorded and can be watched again for clarification/study.

Lectures are informative and enjoyable. He has an amazing understanding of the material and is very helpful in understanding the concepts. The book is nice but not needed. If you do the work and study, it shouldn't be a problem. Does curve tests but don't rely on that to pass. One of the best teachers at FSU Panama City.

Dr. Hadi is an amazing professor and an equally great individual. His lectures are always engaging and informative well beyond what is required. Connections to external practices and uses for course material are abundant without detracting from what is being covered. He is always willing to help with homework as well as provide advice for success

Dr. Hadi is hands down one of my all time favorites. He is able to take complex and difficult topics and teach them in ways that are easy to follow and comprehend. If you don't understand something the first go around, don't worry, he will work a dozen more problems to hammer home the concepts, while encouraging questions to further the discussions

Not only with this course give you an appreciation for the power industry, but it lays out the foundational groundwork for engineers entering this field to be successful. Dr. Hadi has a way of boiling all of this high-concept work into tangible models while also respecting the derivations that make them work. If possible, take this course!

This is a very challenging class, but if you put the time in Dr Hadi will make sure you get through it. If you want to learn take this class, if you want to better your GPA with no work, take IDK a basket weaving class.

The BEST professor FSU PC has for engineering. Knows the subjects and is able to explain them extremely well. Tests are tough but fair. If you put in the work and are trying, he will help you out. Relates well to students and can be approached about any problems, even if they aren't related to his class.

This professor was the highlight of my experience in the Electrical Engineering program at FSU. He has a deep mastery of the material and is passionate about lectures and labs. Works hard to refresh the lectures every time the course is taught, improving continuously. Be prepared to work hard, but you will learn a lot.

Dr. Hadi is very knowledgeable and enthusiastic about the subject material. His lectures are well prepared and consist of working several examples, which I find to be very helpful to learning the new concepts. He is very approachable so do not hesitate to ask homework questions outside of class. I recommend taking any course taught by Dr. Hadi.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Dr Hadi is by FAR one of the best instructors that I have ever had. I've never felt that I couldn't approach him about ANY situation. He is incredibly caring and truly wants his students to succeed. He will give relatively difficult tests, but at the end of the semester he will work with you to make sure you succeed.

Dr. Hadi is easily one of the most engaging and exciting professors I have ever had. He is extremely passionate about teaching and it really shows. If I could have him for every class I would absolutely do it. He provides clear rubrics and the expectations do not come from left field, rather, he is up front about what you are expected to know.

Outstanding professor! Every class that I've had so far with him though (EEL 3111, EEL 3112), has been top tier and if you're willing to pay attention and put in the work you will learn a lot. This class was no exception. Professors that have this passion for teaching, energy, and level of competency all in one package seem rare.

Every lecture has a pourpuse, and each lab will corrilate to those. Very important to attend class if you wanto to succed as well as HW. Dr. Hadi is always available in office or email. A proffessor that makes you work for it, but in turn that makes you learn 10X's more! Always inergetic in lectures, not boring. had for multiple classes.

The EE Labs at FSU can be tough, especially with the requirement to write so many papers, but Dr. Hadi places the emphasis on hands-on learning using actual circuits and instruments. The labs really reinforce the lectures and drive home concepts. It helps to take him for both. He is very active in the lab, helping students troubleshoot their issues

The subject matter is difficult, but you couldn't ask for a better professor. He knows the material inside and out and easily answers any questions you might have. He is passionate and enthusiastic about everything he teaches and it shows in the lectures. Get ready to work hard though - you wanted to be an engineer, right?
Worst professor I ever had.

Dr. Hadi is a FANTASTIC teacher. I have had him for both Circuits I and II, and he went above and beyond to assist me! And Power was no different, but do not think it is an "easy class." It is a very VERY rich course and requires you to study, take notes, do the homework, and pay attention. Interesting class, lots of real world application, 10/10.
Awful professor. Sometimes it's hard to understand what he is talking about.

Dr. Hadi is an awesome teacher and is always excited to teach his subject areas. Honestly the best classes Ive ever had are with him and Ive learned the most in his classes all the way from circuits 1 to advanced electronics. Hes very adept at getting the material across and will go out of his way to help you understand it.

reviews.comment: Dr Hadi is by far my favorite professor at FSU PC. I have had this professor for essentially all of the hardware classes in the electrical engineering program. His lectures are always interesting, engaging, and filled with useful information. He typically discusses useful algorithms and topics that are not presented in the book. SO GO TO CLASS!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Inspirational--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Dr. Hadi shows respect for all his students. Lectures are upbeat and very thorough on the subject matter. Very passionate about teaching the course material. Good at making the connections between theory and applications. Exams are fair and reflect lecture material. Overall an excellent professor and person. Would recommend any of his courses.

Dr. Hadi gives concise, meaningful and useful lectures. Although at the beginning we were only graded by exams, he has moved on to giving assignments that helps grades bump up. For an undergrad class, he does want you to do work especially in labs. He gives insight to how these knowledge are used in the field.

Great professor who is always excited about the course material. Lectures are focused around problem-solving. Three exams a bulk of the grade, along with the frequent take-home assignments. Most assignments pair up with lab content. Overall best class of the semester.

reviews.comment: Awesome professor! His lectures were by far my favorite at FSU.

reviews.comment: Really good professor who knows his stuff and wants to make sure the students genuinely do too. He places a lot of emphasis on labs and wants to make sure students see the theory that they're learning in action. Lectures are very fast paced and passionate so it can be difficult to keep up -- good luck trying to take thorough notes.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Dr. Hadi does a fantastic job. He really cares about his students and he wants them to succeed. He will tailor his lectures to the needs of individuals without hindering the progress of other students. Communicates well. Very helpful during office hours. The material can be difficult but its senior level EE, its all going to be difficult.

Dr. Hadi has his courses well organized. His lectures are easy to follow and very participatory. He answers questions well and when you need additional guidance he is readily available outside of class. I recommend Dr. Hadi for any class.

The lectures are very important, and taking notes is the best way to do well in his class. However he is very fast paced so keeping up while taking notes can be difficult. His labs are very helpful and he makes an effort to work with every student in the lab to ensure that everyone understands.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

reviews.comment: Fantastic professor who genuinely cares about students. Always willing to help outside of class and responds quickly to emails. Lectures are very fast paced and difficult to take notes.

Dr. Hadi is a very involved and caring professor. Note taking is almost essential in his classes, but he is very straightforward about tests and assignments. He is very involved in the lab, and makes sure every students feels good about the material.
One of the best professors I've ever had! I loved how we did lots of group work and group thinking. I learn more when there's open conversation. 10/10!

I came into this class knowing barely anything about culture and communication. This course made me realize how important culture is in daily life.Dr. H knows his stuff, plus his teaching style motivated us to learn. No exams, but the activities and projects were interesting and challenging. The class was a positive experience;highly recommended!

This was a very interesting class.Professor Hoffman showed us different kinds of methodologies and explained those with different cases  in various businesses. The final project was demanding, but we were able to apply what we had learned.
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Great, laid back, understanding. Mandatory attendance but super flexible if you run late. Answers emails quickly, accessible for lab reports beforehand if u need help. Lenient grader but expects you to put in effort. Cares about his students, open to questions. Lab was a breeze w him.

Stephen is SUCH a good TA. He makes it easy for you to succeed and actually cares. He will tell you his personal experiences in his career and is always there for advice when it comes to your life and career. He is teaching B+B next year, and I would take him. Provides GREAT feedback for things like lab reports and is great at answering emails!
Steven is such a king and was an AMAZING TA!!! He really cares and shares his own personal life experience to help students. I always enjoyed going to lab every week - highly recommend him!!!

I've taken Professor Hay for two semesters now (currently 5039, not 5669), and I have found his classes to be some of the most interesting I've taken. Not an overly easy class but if you care about the material, which he makes it very easy to do, then the class isn't too challenging. You have to put in the work but he is not too tough of a grader.

Our grades only consisted of discussion posts and tests. Took this class since it was online, however it ended up being more work than my other classes. The discussions also were about 3-4 paragraphs and were nearly impossible to get a 5/5 on. Also, the tests are open book but only thirty minutes and very specific.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Exams are open note, but timed so you have to read the material before taking it. LOTS of reading. He does not give individual feedback on discussion posts, so its hard to know what you did wrong and need to do better next time. He just sends a mass message saying what was commonly done wrong, so you have to guess what you did wrong.

This professor taught the class well. He made sure to explain in detail assignments and papers that were due. However, he was a tough grader and didn't believe in curving grades. He did provide an extra credit opportunity towards the end of the semester. Be prepared to do a lot of writing which is commensurate with being in grad school.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: Self-control, crime, & cj with Hay was probably my favorite course this semester. The class is composed of 4 discussions and 4 exams. The discussions are about once a month and the exams are timed but multiple choice. The class sounds intimidating but if actually dedicate time towards the class and are interested in the topic, youll be golden.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Dr Hay is an awesome teacher. Gives really clear feedback after each week /test and tells you what exactly he is looking for. Love the way he breaks his course down. Tons of reading but worth it. Tough grader.

STEHPHEN HILL IS FIRE !!!!!! He's a fair grader and lets you know what's going to be on mini exams. There is a textbook but its online and you rarely need it, everything you will need is in the lecture slides that he posts and the homework. In addition, he drops one of your lowest mini-exam scores. Overall, he's a lovely guy!

This class was difficult not because the content was hard to comprehend, it was because Dr. Hill would provide minimal guidance to succeed in this course. Also, he would give new questions on the examinations that he didn't teach us about and we would have to figure out to solve them during the exam. This isn't a complain these are the facts.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: My grade is around (A - B-) depends on how good or bad I did on the final. I don't pay attention to zoom lectures cause I can't with the stupid small screens but that's my problem. Just do homework and study and you should pass. If you have questions go to office hours they are very helpful. People complain too much about him for no reason tbh.
reviews.rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Dr. Hill is an excellent lecturer. He does lots of demonstrations and is really good at breaking down the material. His tests are somewhat difficult. Averages are usually D's, but you are graded on a lot of other stuff in this class

He's a smart man but don't bother asking questions during class. He is so incredibly sassy for no reason. This class isn't easy for most, and the tests are HARD (averages were in the 50s). But there's ways to get additional points through homework, participation, attendance, etc.
