I despise his teaching methods. He tells you that the textbook is wrong, but refuses to give you what he believes to be the correct answers. He prides himself in the fact that no one passes his finals with anything higher than a C- and his weekly quizzes are ridiculous because he delights in trick questions. Avoid Lindsay at all cost.

Dr. Lindsay gives fun,lively lectures that bring the subject to life for those who really want to learn it!  His tests are always lecture and book-driven.  Take notes, read the book,and you'll do well.  Dr. Lindsay will do ALL HE CAN to help you succeed!

I've taken Intro to Advertising, Public Relations Writing, and Disneyology with him.  He is very clear on expectations and tests what he lectures.  I made high A's in all of the classes I've taken with him.

I would reccomend him. He makes all of his classes and the content stimulating. He is very clear in what is expected and will clarify and elaborate on any questions. If we graded teachers, I would give him an A+:)

This guy should retire. He is lousy at teaching and his objectives for the class ate not clearly stated. He expects the student to know the information he wants without going over the material in class. He also isn't very open to new ideas.
No Comments

He is literally the best. He always assumes the best of you, trusts his students, and tries to make their lives easier. He teaches Hebrew in a way that you can actually understand because he makes sure everyone is caught up and on the same page. He lets you know exactly what's on the test or quiz in advance, so it's actually really hard to fail.
Dr. Levenson assigns a lot of work, but he is a great teacher and gives you a lot of chances to make up work. Will take your calls or texts about Hebrew, and really wants you to do well.
He is the coolest human being EVER. I wish he could teach all of my classes

Over email he can seem rude but he had me call him and he is the absolute nicest person, he doesn't want you to fail. I need this class to graduate and couldn't have done it without his help and my TA and they would have been justified in not giving it to me as I was extremely behind. Work is pretty easy, hope for a nice TA, don't fall behind.

My one advise for this class is be prepared to write your hands off. At the beginning of the course it was tedious and dreadful, however, the material was pretty interesting and the writing comes to you like clockwork. By the end of the semester you find yourself writing 10-13 page papers effortlessly or at least a bit faster than when you started.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: Even though he was my professor, I never interacted with Kelley directly because the TA's literally run this course. The only thing Kelley would do to reach out is tell us how late work is not accepted and he will not change this policy for anyone. The TA's all differed in how they graded assignments so it is purely luck to have a good TA.

Dr. Kelley is not so much the problem as is his TAs. The TA I was given is extremely inconsistent on her grading and provides little to no feedback, even when you ask her. I made good grades on the assignments, but a lot of other people received horrible grades for small things, like wrong headings. The tests are easy, open book and not timed.

There are two discussion boards a week, two 1500 word essays and two exams. the course material is not hard at all. but Dr Kelley himself acts as if he doesn't care about his students or the course. any time I or one of my peers have reached out to him he seems to reply in the most condescending and uncaring way possible. TA's run the course.

This class isnt hard. There are 2 discussion posts per week&then 2 research papers & two online tests. I rated him so horribly is because of his TA's and his responses. He is rude and doesn't care about his students. His TA's grade horribly and when we ask him to look over it he says no and his responses are always rude. He needs to care more.

This class is super easy, you'll have to try to fail, honestly. However, it is NOT a Crim elective, only upper division writing, so beware. Kelley is HORRIBLE, rude or doesn't respond when you email him instead of your assigned TA's. Ur TA's are hit or miss, so they determine your grade pretty much. Good class if you want to boost your GPA though.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: Just do yourself a favor and take this class. This is one of the easiest classes at FSU.

Graded by a TA. Two 5-10 page research papers about whatever criminal case you want; graded ROUGH on first draft (FD) but easily on the final submission (F) (ex. 73 FD to a 97F). Two very EASY 25Q exams. Weekly 350w min discussion posts about chapter with (2) 150w min responses. It does get a tad overwhelming at first glance but just pace yourself.
reviews.rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Kelley is an amazing professors, just complete everything on time and you'll get an A!!

Took this class online and the discussions every single week were very annoying but not hard. 2 essays that were pretty easy follow the rubric and you'll get an A. Don't miss any discussions because they are a big part of your grade. Exams were easy and can be found online. Class is pretty straightforward and actually pretty interesting.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: This class is heavily writing based as it satisfies the upper division writing credit. 350 word discussions due every week, 2 online exams, and 2 research papers. Pretty straight-forward class. He says you need both textbooks but I only ever used the Media and Crime book and ended with an A+. Fair grader and responsive.

His classes are very easy as I have taken 2 of them now, but despite how much I like his classes I think he is a horrible professor. Everytime I have emailed him a question, which has only been a few, he has answered so nasty and condescending and it is so unprofessional. As a class in whole, I recommend it, but as for himself he is rude and unprof

Dr. Kelley is one of the best professors at FSU! He is very helpful and his assignments are very straightforward. We had weekly discussions, 2 exams, and a research paper. He is an easy grader so it should be an easy A! I am taking another one of his classes next semester because I enjoyed his class so much!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Dr. Kelley is by far the best professor I've had at FSU and the CCJ 3032 class was amazing. He makes the class fun and interesting and I always looked forward to what we would be doing the next week, which has never happened to me before. The class is so interesting, I definitely recommend it. The work is clear and easy, and he grades very fairly.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Online course . The course expectations are clear, the work load is completely manageable especially for a graduate level class. Each week you'd either have a brief or peer review & discussion post due. Dr. Kelley was a very fast grader, provided feedback after each assignment, and did not take off for late assignments. Great professor!

I took the Criminal Justice class in person and there was NO point in going to class. All him and his assistant do is talk about stories they have and nothing about the actual content needed for tests. Discussion boards every week, midterm and final, one final paper written on the Casey Anthony case.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Took Crime in Media class online this semester, super easy always got 100% on weekly discussion boards. 2 or 3 tests, 2 research papers that I scored a 93 and 98. Need the textbooks to get all of the necessary information from bulletpoints on the powerpoint slides. Great class!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Took his class online and it was an easy A. discussion board every week, midterm and final are taken at home. 2 research papers on a topic of your choice + feedback given after you post the draft, then you repost the final draft. If you miss a discussion board you can make it up any time before the final. You need the books for the DB's and tests.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Would take again

reviews.comment: I took it online. 2 exams, 1 research paper and weekly discussion boards will make up your grade. It's a fairly easy A as long as you stay on top of everything!
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I took his Serial Killer class and it wasn't the best I've taken  online but definitely not the worst. Mid-term & final are 10 repetitive questions, use your discussion posts, so the 5-10 pages aren't bad (mine typically was 5-6 pages). Discussion posts were nothing to worry about, short & simple. Tech paper, use the format & don't over think it.
Mid term was 10 essay questions treated like essays with APA formatting and citations. Not responsive.
Easy A or B don't require long discussion answers. Test are durable but you are required to write 3 research papers between 5-10 pages long.

DR. THOMAS KELLEY ONE  OF THE BEST PROFESSORS I HAVE HAD AT FLORIDA STATE TWO EXAMS MID-TERM AND FINAL WHICH IS NOT HARD. RESEARCH PAPER WHICH IS NOT HARD HE PROVIDES ALL THE MATERIAL YOU NEED TO COMPLETE IT. DISCUSSION BOARDS WAS EASY NOTHING TOO MAJOR I LOVE THIS PROFESSOR I WISH I COULD TAKE HIM FOR ALL MY CLASSES.

Dr. Lapointe was one of my favorite professors at Florida State. He truly cares about his students and wants them to learn and achieve great things. He tries to make the class engaging. Some of the best parts of the class was when he had our entire class over for breakfast and when we went to the brain lab in the College of Medicine.

Dr. LaPointe is one of the most inspiring professors I've ever had in my college career. His lectures are always interesting and always lightens up the mood in class to make Neuro fun and easy to learn. He requires you to understand the information and truly apply what you're learning; especially since tests are cumulative. Overall, fantastic.
No Comments

Probably the most boring professor I've ever had. Questions on the tests are pretty confusing and unclear. Almost all of them are "which of these statements is true/false" which gets pretty annoying. Must go to class because she leaves blanks in the powerpoints during lecture. I'd recommend taking someone else before her if you can.

The material is not hard, but on the power points she posts, she leaves a bunch of blanks. So if you don't go to class, you'll miss a lot of the info and have to go through with the book to fill in the blanks. She gets very repetitive and almost gives too MANY examples. She's nice and helpful, but her class can be very frustrating and boring.

Funny as hell!(In little ways, she doesn't try hard to be, just really is) Very smart and has interesting stuff to say. But, class can get somewhat boring.. Probably more the material than the proffessor.. Oh and she's very enthusiastic about the subject. Great experience
Attend the class and take her notes. She gives extra credit for showing up. If you take notes and read chapter summaries it's an easy A.

Quizzes due every single Friday with very few exceptions, which got annoying real fast. Having a quiz due the day after Thanksgiving, after we JUST had a project draft due that Monday, was especially baffling and honestly made me lose some respect for him. At least the quizzes were fairly easy.

Pretty straight forward class. 11 quizzes and 3 tests all open book on canvas. The material is pretty easy too. Just take practice quizzes/tests and you will be fine. There is an easy project which he makes you submit in three parts, each part he gives you feedback so you can improve so the final submission you can get 100. Easy A tbh. Take him!!

His class was super easy. He explains concepts in multiple ways so if you don't get it the first time, he will explain it until you do. Very patient and understanding of circumstances. He records all of his lectures and posts them, as well as live streams them on the day of the class. Amazing professor!

Had to take his class online but I followed along with the lectures he posted on Canvas. Good at teaching complex concepts into simpler terms. Overall, learned a lot and made the class easy. Just pay attention in lecture and you'll be ok. Weekly quizzes. 3 exams. Content is relevant to tests and quizzes.

She is the sweetest professor I've ever had. Her class was fun, supportive, and full of joy. She genuinely cares about her students, and I'm really going to miss her. If you get the chance to take her, do it. She's amazing.
Everyone in the class was disappointed by her grades.

One of the best english teachers I've ever had. I also had her for ENC2315 and she was great. She'll teach you alot if you pay attention. In this class we had FUN covering the norse and marvel mythology. It's some of the best fun I've ever had in college. She likes jokes too.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Respected

reviews.comment: Light work load. Don't need a textbook. Assignments are very straight forward. There are 3 speeches and are pretty easy. Not much note taking either.

He comes off as very egotistical in his writing/emails but nice in his video lectures, never met in person. Very picky grader for no reason. If you don't do one of the assignments you fail the class. Class is manageable but annoying

He is a decent professor. If you are disciplined, this class isn't too bad. A lot of his assignments are pretty easy, and his exams aren't awful if you study the textbook that he made. He isn't the best professor if you aren't great with online classes, so beware of that. He's not super understanding, but I would still take a class with him again.

Red flag when a prof makes you buy their own book. Most content really is common sense, but the assignments are not related at all and the TAs are sooo picky. Book says speeches don't have to be perfect but you'll get points off for not "looking around enough" when talking to a camera. Exams are super easy. Prof waves his hands too much in videos.

Dr. Trammell is a nice dude and he truly does mean well. He's just a little odd in his mannerisms. Work is pretty common sense but you still should watch the lectures. You can't afford to miss any work. He also makes you buy his book which is a repeat across multiple other books he wrote for his classes. Not a hard class just don't be lazy.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He's a really nice guy and means well. He'll give you a thousand opportunities to try to get better at what you're doing. If you don't study you will fail. Just put in the amount of effort necessary to succeed. You can't miss any assignment or you'll fail.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Michael Trammell makes the class harder than it needs to be. His recorded lectures are very unclear at times and are not the best quality. His messages and writing often times have a very detrimental tone to them which can come off very demeaning. Much of what is on the exams come from the textbook which he wrote and cost students an extra $70.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This class is normal l. It has papers, speeches, and tests. What makes my rating lower are little things. I emailed my TA about grading and got no response for a week. I emailed Dr. Trammel and got a rude response even when in my original email I stated I contacted my TA already. 2nd, for every email he sends out, you receive two of them. Annoying.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: The class wasn't difficult, but Trammell seemed to have no clear way of lecturing. Took off major points if you misspelled his name and had a section completely unrelated to the content of the class. He wrote the textbook but don't buy it before class, he will want you to get the target packet which is a lot cheaper. do the reading!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: His class is boring, he frequently states overly obvious things and occasionally contradicted himself.  I've been told there are better teachers for Business Communications

Pretty nice dude. Lets you know what is expected of you. Not a very snappy dresser though. Leave the button up shirts with no collar in the 80s please. Other than that, he is a pretty cool dude, though he may not come across as such on the first day or so.
Will help you if you try, if you come to class and pay attention you will do well.  If you read the book (which he wrote) you should be making an A.

Literally didn't go to class and got over 100. She curves and you barely have to study to get an A. Every exam has at least 5 mistakes, which is super distracting and disruptive while you're trying to talk the test. She's very hard to listen to in lecture because she's pretty all over the place.

By far one of the best teachers I have ever had at FSU.  I had her for clinical psych and she literally made me want to do it for a profession. I slacked off on the first test but if you go to class and listen and then do the study guides she gives you, there is no way you wont get an A.
Her class is EASY, she gives study guides, take her!
Professor Nakamura is kind, helpful, and respectful. She tries to find many ways to teach concepts in this class. Her lectures are very straight-forward, and she does a lot to help you succeed.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Caring

reviews.comment: Very disappointed that you don't get your grades back reasonably on your weekly assignments. One thing wrong will snowball into weeks of missed points because of it. Hard to track down, hard to schedule time with. Instructions are hard to understand, she is friendly but explains things to you like you should already know them.

Do NOT take professor Nakamura for chemistry lab. She is not helpful and hardly ever knows what's going on in class. Her instructions never make any sense, and she is very hard to track down. Also, be prepared to not receive grades until months after turning in the assignment. If you have the choice, take a different lab professor.
I would email her questions, and she wouldn't answer my emails. The rare times she did she would just say look at the book.

She means well. But as far as helping you, she is pretty much useless. She will show you a worked out problem and say something along the lines of "that's the answer because it is". She is hard to understand and she doesn't understand your questions. Over all, she is a very bad teacher.

Had her for numerous classes. Extremely fun, helpful, and concerned for students education. Also at the small college she is an excellent advisor and assisted me many times during my transfers to and from FSU. An overall great person who care for her students. Powerpoints are very helpful and therefore test are very easy. Projects easy! Great Lady!
Excellent Teacher...Professor of the Year 8 times for a reason!!
Dr. Dexter is a great teacher! Her classes seem to more difficult than last semester. She has great stories and examples and makes class fun!

Entertaining in the classroom, helpful, and nice.  This class wasn't the easiest I have taken with her but you can definetly get an A if you study well for the exams.  This class also had a required group presentation and paper about a product and its history.  Overall, a good class.
Uses powerpoints no book! Take this during the summer and you'll get an A. Make sure you spend some time on your paper. She takes off for all grammer errors.
Nice lady. Clear instructions, layman's language, great sense of humor, understanding. Just all around great person. Hint! Take the project seriously!
Awesome....very easy!
Mrs. Dexter is a very nice lady with a great sense of humor. She makes the class fun. I will def take her again! Easy class and I didn't buy the book! =)

He is the best professor for Chemistry I've ever had. so caring + wants u to succeed. He is a fair grader + answers any questions. feedback is very helpful and labs were awesome with him. attend lectures bc you get points for answering questions. he gives a helping sheet during exams with the periodic table AND formulas!! BEST CHEM PROF FSU HAS!!!

Overall a great guy, very thoughtful and would respond to messages. He will work with you as long as you work with him. If you are interested in chemistry, he will try his best to showcase how fun this science can be.
Incredibly nice and helpful professor. Was absolutely amazing at explaining the topics and extremely willing to help and answer questions. He is obviously passionate about what he does.

Dr. Ray at first was very kind and easy to read, or so I thought. continuing through the semester and after the first test, I realized that there was no way I was going to pass this class. he talks in class about things that won't e on the test and pulls test questions out of nowhere. what he teaches has nothing to do with the tests. DONT TAKE HIM.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: STAY AWAY FROM THIS CLASS THIS GUY IS RIDICULOUS DOESNT ANSWER EMAILS AND HIS TEST ARE INSANELY HARD FOR AN INTRODUCTORY METEROLOGY CLASS BIGGEST REGRETS IN MY LIFE

Sweet P. Ray... Nice man, very caring. But as a professor, it is very hard to stay focused with him. He does ramble, isn't the best with grammar (he does disclose this on the first day), and he doesn't teach from textual powerpoints, but rather pulls pictures from the textbook and talks about them. Tests are curved. Be ready to read textbook.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--Caring

reviews.comment: When you first get into class your like already we got a funny teacher, then as the semester rolls on he goes off on the same tangents and they get more and more boring til you are eventually falling asleep in class, i sat in the 2nd row also and he is just a really bad teacher, PLEASE do yourself a favor and switch out,if you cant then getthe book

He is a very nice guy, but a horrible teacher.Unfortunately, you could not comprehend anything he said in class, and I sat in the 2nd row!His tests were impossible and made me want to jump out a window. He goes off on tangents during lecture, talks too fast, and talks too low ( he's a mumbler)! Most people dumped the notes from class and got the no
Get out QUICK!

Great teacher! One of the best teachers I have had in college. She is really nice and makes class interesting. Nothing too hard from her. Weekly quizzes after class. No test. Super short paper for a final. And easy project. Take her class!

Excellent class with awesome discussion! I've taken quite a few of Dr. P's classes, and she consistently provides the correct balance between "fun" and "learning". (She started this class with a Beyonce video.) An excellent professor, doesn't ask for excessive amounts of work - but you WILL learn something if you're in her class!
I took Dr. Polick's Psych of Women course, and it was the best course I have had at FSU. Me and some others actually looked forward to this class each week. Take it if possible!

Dr. Polick is the best professor EVER!! I learned soo much from her course, she is a amazing professor, and really loves her students, she can make anyone laugh!!! Exams are pretty easy if go to class and study! TAKE HER if possible!!
I love her as a professor. She is so fun and so sweet, makes even boring classes enjoyable. Great sense of humor; if you have a choice, TAKE Dr. Polick!

Took Dr.Polick for Child Psychology. Classes are very interesting, she has a great sense of humor, and she uses awesome videos to give examples. Class is never boring. She has a great personality. I really learned a lot. Lots of reading but the quizzes are pretty straight forward and only 1 easy paper.Highly recommend this professor.

Prof. Renfro is one of the best professors I have ever had. She is a very kind and jolly person who you can tell loves her profession.  she gives out extra credit for tests during a lot of the recitations. literally copy down the entire unit slide deck onto the piece of paper you get. Doing this, I averaged above 102% on all of my tests

She is a pretty good teacher and I hate science but I somewhat enjoyed this class. Her exams are kinda tough but going to recitations helps a ton (especially before exams we played Jeopardy for extra credit points). She gives plenty of extra credit and doesn't make the class impossible if you're willing to try.

Anyone who gave her a bad review is probably not the brightest bulb in the pack. I didn't give much effort in this class and got an A. She provides extra credit questions on every exam and they are all multiple choice except for the 4th exam with two free responses at the end. She is very kind and is clearly passionate about biology.
the person that gave a 5 star rating was cap

Lectures are a snooze, tests are riddles and have no clear answer. She knows the test questions are worded terribly so you do not get your tests back and she does not post the keys!
Professor renfro hates to be held accountable. Take the course with someone else if you can for sure.

I went into this class thinking that it would be easy........ I was very wrong. Her lectures are so easy compared to her tests that it blows my mind. When I initially saw exam 1 I was sick to my stomach and it continued to be that way for the next exam.

Though her tests aren't easy, she is an amazing lecturer. She clearly knows what she's talking about and is able to answer any questions. She responds to emails quickly, as well. Overall, as long as you attend lectures and pay attention in class, you should do fine.

Dr. Roach is an amazing professor, and her class has been one of my favorites at FSU. She genuinely cares about each one of her students and she will meet with you and make sure you have the resources you need to succeed. Her class is not hard as long as you pay attention, and I really could not recommend her class enough!

Professor Roach was absolutely phenomenal. Her video lectures were perfect and all quizzes/assignments given in class matched course material and were never too difficult. Final paper was interesting with clear instructions and she was always quick to put in grades. Absolutely loved her and the class.

Professor Roach was so good at lecturing I actually wanted to go to class, she clearly knows what shes talking about and can answer any questions you might have in class. Her assignments are straightforward and easy and her tests have a essay question that she gives you beforehand!

My biggest problem was it took forever for work to get back to us graded. Its hard to know where you stand when its exam week and 50% of your grade has not been reported. Most of the quiz answers can be found online. I'm not sure how you would possibly know the answers just from reading the material, however. They are complex questions.
reviews.rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: It was an ok class. Don't worry about buying the textbook. She doesn't reset tests or quizzes because so many people try to cheat. Make sure you keep up with what she posts online and you should do well in the class.

Really a wonderful teacher. She makes sure everyone understands the material and is very relatable. Makes every effort to ensure that everyone has the best chance possible at both learning and receiving a good grade. She made me look forward to coming to school everyday.

this class is so chill, attendance wasn't mandatory, spends the entire time lecturing, zero homework assignments, zero quizzes, grades only based off two midterms and the final, not the best lecturer kinda drags on

Save yourself and dont take this with Dr. Salters. He is a terrible teacher that does not care about the well being and grade of his students. Posts false information of the syllabus and won't correct himself until the last minute. He is condescending, unhelpful, and don't expect to actually learn anything. Take online.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: BEST TEACHER EVER
His lectures were not helpful atall. He doesnt do any examples in class and takes 7 weeks to grade your test

Telotte explains things in formulas. If you have trouble understanding new concepts purely mathematically then the lectures are as good as useless. He just rambles on his pretyped notes in class. If you're are lucky he may explain one of the problems on the notes. He has never worked a problem in class. Get ready to learn compressors and turbine
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--GROUP PROJECTS

reviews.comment: Dr. Telotte is incredibly helpful. His knowledge and experience in the field are outstanding. He is a reasonable man and a good person to talk to and take advise from. I had two classes with him this semester and I have to say that I truly loved the way he taught my courses. Be aware that he knows how to test the principles he teaches.
No Comments
No need to buy book. Grades fairly and allows resubmissions if you need to. You don't need to be an art person to find this course easy.
Fair grader and allows for some grammar mistakes. Don't need textbook.
Really good guy, keeps the class interesting and always gives second chances. Pretty easy to get an A in the class if you put in a little effort. Super hot
He loves what he does and you an tell by how much cares about his students.
Pick someone else! This little kid doesn't know how to teach! Email him with a question and he'll make you feel stupid for asking. Nothing positive to say, very pessimistic, arrogant, and miserable.
Knowledgable, provides good course material, cool hipster guy.

While this class was somewhat difficult, partly because his accent is a little strong, Mr. Sub greatly appreciates you stopping by during his office hours if you need help or just to introduce yourself. He also encourages the use of ACE tutoring center. If you take advantage of these things, you'll be fine! He wishes success for all his students.
If you are not naturally good at math do not take him for a teacher because you will need constant tutoring and help. He doesn't even give all the examples that you need to take quizzes and tests.
His class is extremely easy. With math you basically have to practice a lot on your own and make sure you do all of the practices available.

This class is SO easy as long as you show up to class...and 85% of the class never shows up. He is a little difficult to understand, but he does a good job of giving clear examples. He isn't the most helpful when you email him questions, but if you take the time to go to his office hours he will sit down and explain concepts.
You can never understand anything he says.  If you're good at math, you can pass.

I really enjoyed this course! As long as you actually read the chapters and apply yourself, you will pass with an A. The exams come from the homework assignments - so just create a study guide from the questions and study it, you'll do fine. Would definitely take a class by this professor again.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: One of the best professors in the business school. Has a genuine care for all students and wants to see you do well in life. Class is very easy but I would encourage attending lecture as Woodyard's stories are the best. One of the smartest professors I have met and a great person to know. Would recommend his class and meeting him for OH.
An absolute layup of a course. Very clear grading criteria and expectations. The homework is easy; discussion posts are easy, exams are word for word from the homework assignments...
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: He is a lawyer, caring and incredibly professional. I am still in his course but I am impressed by his demeanor, understanding and knowledge. He is PHENOMENAL~!

Never written a review, but I feel like Professor Woodyard deserves it. He's very straightforward and covers all the material in class. I took summer express A so I was graded on just 3 tests. Answers came straight from the study guides in the book. He takes the time to learn your name and you as a person. Stress-free class but learned a lot!!
reviews.rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Respected--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This is a good introductory course for Real Estate - especially for non-majors. He isn't the best lecturer, but all or most of the content in the exams come directly from the study guide that is required for the class. He does keep track of attendance. Overall, he's a sweet guy who cares about his students.
Really chill guy, we had 3 exams and then some online work (super easy and didn't take much time). He's a great lecturer, and he always has an answer even for really specific legal questions.

The best professor. He is very knowledgeable and has a lot of stories from his career that are interesting and help you learn the content better. He genuinely cares about his students and wants them to succeed. Exams were pretty easy and questions came straight from the homework. Would take this class again 100%.

The class was fully online, at your own pace. He posts pre-recorded lecture videos along with PowerPoint. The class consists of homework on Cengage, 3 Exams (entirely based off Cengage HW), and 4 Discussion Boards. Super straightforward class, and very doable when working a full-time job. Cengage will teach you everything.

Professor Woodyard is an amazing teacher and person. His lectures were easy to understand, and he really cares about you learning the content. Would definitely take another class of his, and if you can you should.

There is a $30 book that is used in class and how he creates a lot of his exam questions. 4 tests are the entire course, and he does an exam review before each one and tells you what you need to know. You can miss up to 4 classes and he takes attendance, but the class rarely takes the entire time period. Such a sweet guy and a relatively easy A.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Giant sweetheart. Have to get a $30 study guide but has everything you need for the class including practice questions which are often the test questions. Lectures are kinda boring but he takes attendance, can only miss 4 days a semester.

Professor Woodyard was the best professor I have had at FSU. He is extremely knowledgeable in Real Estate and teaches from personal experience. 4 exams come from the study packet. He is super nice and will sit down with you if you are struggling with concepts. He did start taking attendance mid-semester but if you go to class this won't affect you.

Professor Woodyard is by far the best professor I have had at FSU. His class consists of 4 exams where the questions come from the study packet. If you study you will get a good grade. I have gotten so much out of this class. He is very knowledgeable in Real Estate and Law and genuinely cares about his students. I would 100% recommend taking him.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He doesn't know how to use canvas. Exam dates are wrong and he never sends announcements. Going to class is pointless bc he gives a "study packet" and just recycles the same questions for exams, which are the only assignments. Halfway through the semester he will start taking attendance, so be careful.

Class is really easy. All homework was completed online by a learning program called cengage. 90% of exam questions were from the homework verbatim. No papers or projects. Tests are easy multiple choice but with honorlock. I never watched a single lecture after the first test. Hes knowledgeable but tends to ramble in reviews.

Mr. Woodyard is a great guy who uses fun examples from his life while teaching. He still practices law, so he teaches because he wants to not because he needs to. The exams are very straightforward, as he tells you what you need to know.

He is one of the most caring professors I've ever had. He is a very busy person who is also very willing to help his students with career stuff. I suggest showing up to the lectures and also getting to know him, he and his lectures are full of amazing and hilarious stories from his career. I've learned a lot from having him as a professor.

Dr Woodyard is a great guy. You can tell he genuinely cares about his students. Attendance was optional (he posted recordings), but he always gave us relevant life advice in terms of real estate. The only grades are the 4 exams, but the questions literally come word-for-word from the book/study guide and he tells you which ones you need to know.

Woodyard was an awesome professor who really cared about his students. As long as you read the study guide and know the questions at the end of the chapters you should get an A. Although not a necessity to pass, I would also recommend attending his lectures as he teaches stuff that is valuable and useful for life. 10/10 recommend.

He is one of my favorite professors, and I even added real estate as my major because of this class! He is honestly so funny and kind, and I was always excited to go to his class every week. Most of the exam questions are straight from the target packet, so if you just study that, you'll get a passing grade.

I love professor Woodyard!! I don't know if he realizes how funny he is but he can be a character. He is very sweet and really wants his students to do well. The test questions come from the textbook and he tells you which ones will be on there. He takes some questions from his lectures but its usually things that he spends a lot fo time one.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria--Caring

reviews.comment: Definitely take this guy if you get a chance! He has so much life experience in the real estate subject, and honestly, almost made me want to switch my major to real estate. He allows 4 free absences, but it is definitely worth it to show up. The exams are fairly easy, most of the questions are straight from the chapter multiple choice.

Great guy who knows a lot about what he is teaching, sometimes almost too much. All you need is the study guide and almost all the test questions are word for word from the questions in that. Graded on 4 tests only, but takes away points for more than 4 references. You will learn a lot from Dr. Woodyard if you actually go to class and pay attention

You have to show up because you can only miss 4 classes. He uses the target packet for his class, but the textbook is not necessary. Study the practice questions in the target packet because he uses those on the test (sometimes slightly alters the wording). Go the day before the test because he goes over exactly what is on it. 4 tests, each 25%

Professor Woodyard is extremely smart, professional, and truly cares about his students. Class lectures come from the textbook with homework each week based on the chapters. The first two exams are not too hard as long as you show up to class and review the chapters. The final is easier and comes straight from the homework questions. Take Woodyard!
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Easily one of the best professors I have had at FSU. He has tons of real world experience that you cannot get from a textbook. Truly knows his material, and has interesting lectures.

He does ramble, but he has a lot of experience as a lawyer and on the board of directors in a few companies. He uses his wealth of experience to add to the lectures even though he just reads from the textbook. Literally just go to class, take good notes, and do the homework (which you can't get wrong). He's an easy A, but really tired during OHs.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I loved Woodyard. He gives you a clear understanding for what will be on every test. Lectures are not too bad because he brings in his real life stories as a lawyer. The homework is so easy, if you don't get a 100 you're just lazy. He offers extra credit on every exam but i got A's on them without it. I would take him again!!
Dr. Woodyard is one of the best teachers I've had at FSU. Lectures are very interesting and tests are straightforward. Mandatory attendance. Show up to class and read the book and you will do well.

He is one of the most caring prof I've ever had! I got a 100 on this class. He gives extra credit and his exams are really easy (multiple choice). If you read the book and attend to classes , you can get an easy A. He is very knowledgeable and gives real life cases that he has taken and solved. You would definitely love this class!

His exams aren't that bad because the questions he gives you come straight from the book. However, the last exam he said was not cumulative BUT IT WAS. He also wouldn't respond to emails and if he did, he would have an attitude. Glad that this class is over.

Right now you're trying to convince yourself you can wake up for an 8am. Trust me it is as PAINFUL on the first day as it is on the last. He's a good professor, big ego, but fair and you'll actually learn a decent amount if you go to class. Don't go and you'll fail because attendance is required. Good luck
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: You need to go to class to pass. He tells students the exact questions that will be on the exam and lists both terms and tricks that will be on the exam. He teaches you how to be successful in life and how to use what we learn in class to your advantage. I highly recommend him I have learned so much and will be taking his other courses.

Woodyard is one of the best professors you'll ever have. He applies the material to realistic situations and is adored by his class. I have so much respect for this man because he genuinely cares about his students, gives fantastic advice, and is one of the most down to earth people I know. If you can, take his class, I promise you'll enjoy it.

THE BEST PROFESSOR HANDS DOWN AT FLORIDA STATE. He has an extensive background on all the courses he teaches. I have had him for Business law and Real Estate and would take him again. Very Kind, Test are fair. He goes out of his way to make sure you pass. LISTEN TO HIS REVIEWS LIKE THEY ARE THE ANSWERS FOR EXAM!
Honestly messed up by not going to class but clutched final test. 4 tests each. Each test is worth 25% of your grade. DO GOOD early on and you'll be fine.

Woodyard is so tenured and respected and you can see he loves what he does. Older guy with a lot of wisdom and experience. Class requires a target copy book with all of the answers to the tests in the back. Practically hands you an A. He can be pompous sometimes and certainly favors athletes. Go to his office often and he'll help you w the tests.
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--Would take again

reviews.comment: Professor Woodyard is worth giving my only rating 3 years into college. He gives great feedback and is such an interesting lecturer. As long as you show him the respect to show up to his classes, he provides everything you need to pass the course, as well as making the course material interesting.

He's the best professor I've had at FSU. The class is at 8am but I didn't mind because the lectures were so interesting! He has realistic examples for every concept, which sparks your interest and helps you understand the subject. He's absolutely brilliant and wants his students to succeed in class and in life overall. Tests are straightforward

Best professor I've had this semester. Really cares about the content and his students (the ones who come and respect him anyway). Most of his tests come directly word for word from his homeworks but he threw in one tough one you actually had to know the content for. Hated having a class at 8am but when you're up this guy isn't bad at all.

Knowledgeable professor. Tests are pulled straight from target pack questions so review those and with enough studying you will land an A. Final grade dependent on 4 tests non cumulative- no outside work. Not the biggest fan of teaching style, as it can be monotonous to say the least. Attendance optional, but if attend he will give xtra cred Qs
reviews.rating_tags: Respected--Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Not a good experience with Dr. Woodyard. Even though I did well in the class,  it felt like an eternity just to get through the semester. I feel that he comes off rude and disrespectful at times. Lots of material to go through.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: Professor Woodyard is one of the best professor's I've ever had. He's so knowledgable and genuinely cares about his students. His required materials is a study guide which has ALL of the answers for the test on it. If you study, you will pass easily.

I enjoyed having a class with Dr. Woodyard, even though the material was a little dry, but it's law so that's expected. Show up to class, pay attention, and you'll do just fine. There is a lot of material, but he makes his tests pretty straight forward. Attendance is taken everyday, so make sure you show up or your grade will suffer.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Professor Woodyard is a man with lots of great stories and experience that anyone can learn from. Classes are lecture based, and he will eventually walk you through some of the questions before the exam. He typically does not let out class early.   I would recommend reading before class, it definitely helps with conceptualizing.

Woodyard literally told us what the material was on the exam was going to be. The exact questions. If you did not get an A in the class it was 1000% percent your own fault. Very direct and very entertaining. Always let class out early, usually between 30-45 minutes early every class. Take Woodyard for an Easy A and also a great professor
Professor Woodyard is an amazing individual. Show up to class, participate in the lecture by being prepared, read the short amount of text necessary and get to know this guy. TAKE WOODYARD.

Woodyard knows what he's talking about and if you actually pay attention in class you will learn! You have to study hard for his test. So STUDY STUDY STUDY and you should be good! He's a great guy over all and will teach you how to make money in the future. Make sure you attend class for his quizzes.

Pretty easy. I got a B with minimal studying. Read the Target Packet and you're good to go. Attendance doesn't help your grade but he takes one percentage point off of your final grade for every absence past three, so show up. As for Dr. Woodyward himself, eh, he's okay. Keeps the class interesting but brags A LOT about how much money he has.

Dr. Woodyard is great. Although his class is at 8am and attendance is mandatory, it is worth it. His gives quizzes but he gives you the answers to them, and he pretty much tells you exactly what will be on the test in his review sessions. He has done a lot in his lifetime and is very knowledgable about the law and real estate. Best prof. I've had
He needs to stick to teaching the real estate course. The law for accountancy class is more difficult than it should and he has trouble presenting the information in an orderly fashion.

One of the sweetest, most intelligent, and caring teachers you will find a FSU. I so highly recommend any class with him. Dr. Woodyard is so knowledgable and personable, and he truly cares about each of his students.

Best teacher at FSU imo, doesnt use textbook. Takes attendance, but lectures are very very entertaining and he'll tell you most of what is on the test. Also, very nice guy and always willing to help and talk to students

Intro to Real Estate- Woodyard is the man, excellent teacher, class is super interesting and he will say know this that will be a question on the test in class. extremely helpful if you go to him. knows anything you need to know and answers to questions. HIGHLY reccommend mr woodyard

I had him for law for accounting. Great teacher! His class is not "easy", it's a great combination between making it possible to get an A and actually learning a lot throughout. Go to his office hours because he can explain things really well that you didn't get in class. He is also very funny. I'm taking him again for Real Estate! Can't wait!

Great class, he is always helpful and nice. He says to even come to him if we need any help in the future with any real estate or legal related issues. Awesome guy and fun class. Don't buy the textbook, you wont use it. Or like I did, buy the book cheap from a student then sell it online for $20 more.lol TARGET PACKET IS IMPORTANT
He's alright. There's ways to do really well in his course. He's a bit arrogant, but you can tell he also cares. Don't have many options, so best to enjoy him and go to class.

Dr. Woodyard is by far one of the best professors teaching at FSU. I took him for REE3043 and BUL3330. Besides being a good teacher, he is a great person to know. His classes are semi challening, but he gives good reviews of whats going to be on the test. TAKE HIM!!

Dr. Woodyard is an amazing guy that is knowledgeable about what he teaches. I took him for BUL3330 & REE3043 and I enjoyed both classes. He gives a pretty good review before each exam (3). If you go to class and take notes you will do amazing. Dr. Woodyard is quite humorous and you would be missing out if you didn't take at least 1 class with him.

Dr Woodyard is an extremely helpful professor. Prior to taking this class I had little desire to take other real estate classes.However due to his interest in helping his students learn, I decided to take a few more Real Estate classes. He made his class very interesting,and enjoyable to attend. He is also a very helpful resource and a nice person!

Dr. Woodyard is a fantastic professor! His method of teaching is to keep it real and relative. You will use the materials, but he adds a real world spin. Once in class his blackberry went off and he looked down and exclaimed "see that's how you make money". He's great. If you need help, you can talk to him. Great resource for future networking!
He is easy and entertaining, BUT if your annoyed by those who play favorites especially to the girls, he's king of that behavior.
I agree with the other comments.  If you want to enjoy your time you spend at school...take as many classes with Dr. Woodyard as you can.
This guy will teach you everything and most importantly how to make money!!!
Dr. Woodyard is an awesome teacher and cares about his students....every single one of them.   Teachers like him make students want to attend class and do well.
No Comments

James Wooland is probably the best college math professor I have ever had and it makes me mad that other people are rating him anything less than 5. Discrete math 2 is very proof heavy but he made it easy to learn, as there were a ton of resources on the course website. Quizzes once a week on recent class material so make sure you don't skip.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Easy content. Mostly just basic word problems and probability & statistics. Can be confusing if you miss a day where he teaches a lot but schedule is on the syllabus. Quiz every week during the lab, a few tests, homework every week. He's a good teacher and you usually won't ever be confused.

Professor Wooland was a great mathematics professor for students who hate mathematics. I am one of those students. While I can say it was my fault I did not receive and A, I easily could have had I went to class. His lectures are very clear and easy to understand. If you're looking for a GenED class to fill math, MGF1106 with Wooland is the move.

If you miss two classes in a row prepare to be lost. Instead of the practice problems online being named after their section and part number, they have vague nicknames like "The Deducer"- which is confusing and annoying, considering the syllabus and his emails on what to studywhat we will be going over go by  Sections and part numbers.
Easiest class ever. 3 attempts at homework. 2 attempts at tests. PRS for extra credit everyday. Go to class and it is impossible to screw up!
Goes over everything, you take exams at least twice, can take homework at least 3 times
No Comments
this class was so freakin easy.
No Comments

Very knowledgeable and is available to speak with you about any subject. Lectures can be long but there is great information. The program that he heads for underwater crime scene is one of a kind and helps to prepare you for the real world. He amps you up so that when you actually do the task is doesn't seem as bad.

Difficult at times but relatively easy to work with. Ive known him for more than 2 years now and on a personal level he is somewhat hardheaded in his ways but truly wants the beat for each of his students. His instructions and expectations are extremely vague so be ready for that. overall hes a good guy with a lot to offer, though hard on you.

Most of the work in these two classes are done inside of class and lab.  Do take good notes and study for quizzes.  He will let you use handwritten notes for quizzes.  You will have to do some assignments outside of class, but they are reasonable.  Also, you might have to give up some of your weekends, but it is time well spent.

Prof. Baber is very understanding and has a real focus on making a connection. If you want to have a great class, be sure to extend the connection by participating as much as possible. While he is lenient, you will have an overall richer experience if you are involved and actively listening. He also knows tons of people in the industry.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Participation matters

reviews.comment: We're assigned DSM's and normal assignments which each have a similar weight to the quizzes. Because of this, it's very difficult to receive a poor grade in the class. Professor Baber is lenient on punctuality depending on your circumstances arriving to class. A solid professor to take if you're interested in the Entrepreneurship program at FSU.
Great professor

Great prof, very responsive and respectful. All online, a total of 6 tests, and that's it for assignments. Everything else is reading and watching videos. Very informative and makes the subjects easy to understand.

I had her when she co-taught this class with Professor Mooney. Her lectures were boring, but you had to pay attention because she did not put any information on the powerpoints slides, only what was spoken in class. This made the quizzes unnecessarily difficult, as a lot of the info was pulled from what she spoke on once in class.

Sweet and funny teacher. A little lecture heavy but she makes it very interesting, I learned new things here. This class has two easy papers and she allows resubmissions if you don't like your grade. Attendance is not mandatory but if you miss notes for the week you might not do well on the weekly quiz. Easy weekly quizzes and final was open note!

Her lectures were great and I enjoyed her as a teacher. This class had 8 grades 6 of which were tests. It wasn't self paced which made it annoying when completing the lectures and tests. I would take detailed notes on all the lectures and I would still score in the 80s for all the exams.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Robbins has engaging lectures, but the exams require extensive note-taking. Assignments are graded strictly, but helpful feedback is available. Unstuck ai made study prep much easier.

This class is rather easy and you shouldn't have an issue getting an A with normal effort, but seriously, type out every. single. word. she says in lectures and reread it 3 times before quizzes. There will, without fail, be at least one question on some extremely specific detail she said once on every quiz. Otherwise not difficult.
An amazing and caring professor. Truly hilarious and makes history engaging. She has such a passion for this class and is always accessible for any questions and reviews essays before you submit!

Dr. Robbins is hilarious and very passionate about what she teaches. I was worried since I have this class online that I may not be able to keep up, but I honestly can say I have been excited to watch Dr. Robbins lectures. The essay graders are a bit harsh, but if you reach out to them they are very helpful in redirecting you, (great for rewrites!)

I don't understand how this professor has such a high grade. She was rude in any emails sent to her, not understanding at all. Quizzes are very difficult, questions are vague. Writing assignments are very harshly graded. Don't take her.
Easy class if you go through the slides and complete all assignments. There are weekly quizzes and the four lowest grades get dropped. There are also two simple essays and 3 main exams.

I took this class online and it truly was a testament of willpower. The lecture videos are unnecessarily long and mostly she tests you on specific, little things she says that half the time aren't even written on the PowerPoints she posts. The material isn't hard to understand, so this isn't a hard class it's just annoying.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Super funny and nice professor! Every week there is a 10-15 question quiz on the lectures. The lectures are powerpoints with her audio recording over it and there can be a lot sometimes. There's two tests and a final and two papers. She grades pretty easy on the papers as long as you follow the rubric. Very easy class to pass if you keep up!

The 2 essays required for this course are graded very harshly with little feedback given for the rewrite. Grading is also very inconsistent when comparing essay grades between peers in the class. I follow the rubric thoroughly yet the graders are very nitpicky. Don't recommend it if you're not an amazing writer/expect to be graded at a 2000 level.

Very enlightening class with interesting, humorous lectures. You need to write down basically everything from the lecture because exam questions are very specific, but otherwise they aren't too hard. The one writing project gives plenty of options and is leniently graded with a rubric. Great choice to fulfill many course requirements at once.

Syllabus is really straight forward and Dr. Robbins makes her expectations clear. Lectures are pretty long but are manageable to finish if spread out and not done last minute. Taking notes on the lectures are important to get good grades on exams, which is pretty much your entire grade. Watch the lectures and take notes and it's an easy A

If you don't write down every single thing she says, you won't get As on the tests. Very test heavy and test questions are often points she stated one time in her two hour lecture. Very sweet lady though, just a ton of notes for a class I was expecting to be easy
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Robbins is great and passionate about what she teaches. Her lectures did tend to be very long. The projects throughout the semester are easy if you read the rubric. Tests are most of your grade.
Amazing teacher, flexible with due dates if needed. Lectures are genuinely informative as per the exams. The projects are lengthy but not mind- numbingly boring.
I took this class online and it honestly is so much work. You really have to write down everything she says and it's too much effort for a gen ed class, take something else.

Dr. Robbins is a great professor who knows her stuff. Lectures are powerpoints with audio over them. Graded on 5 exams and 2 papers (& 2 drafts). As long as you take notes, exams and papers will be very easy. Extra credit available.
reviews.rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: If you hate writing and need to fulfill W req, TAKE THIS CLASS! The writing part was easy and she's clear about grading. The exams, you need to put in the time to take notes on her online lectures and in person lectures, but its not bad. I'd say maybe 2hrs max outside of class time a week. You won't do well if you don't watch and go to  lectures.
reviews.rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Professor Robbins is one of the best I've ever had at my time at FSU. For this class, there are 5 quizzes (No Honorlock), and 2 long-form essays. You need to pay attention to the PowerPoint because the quizzes are all based on them. There is audio to listen to which can be anywhere from 1-10 mins. She is AMAZING otherwise though!!

I never met Professor Robbins since its online, but she seems super nice and always gets back to my emails and is willing to help. The class is graded by very few things and the tests are slightly difficult, but not honor locked which is nice. As long as you listen to all of her ppt with the sound on and write very detailed notes you will get an A.
reviews.rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I took Pam's class last semester and I loved it so much I signed up for her other class this semester even though I don't need it for graduation. Pam is one of the most loving professors i have ever had and she is so respectful even if you have opinions that don't align with hers. She stays as late as you need to help clear anything up. 10/10

I didn't like her. She was cringe and made us watch dumb movies instead of teaching us. She also made us learn about pop culture (boring). That being said, most things are online and easy to take, but I still didn't like her. "What did Ice T say to Bob Dylan during the Civil War?" That's what the class was like.
Lectures were a blast. She is funny and cool. We didn't even have to go to class the second half of the semester.

I loved Dr.Robbins. She was by far my favorite professor that I have had at FSU. I typically despise history but she made the class very interesting. The only outside work we had was watching online lectures and occasionally watching films that were related to the material. All of the tests were open note except the final.

Laziest professor I have dealt with in a long time. There are 4 quizzes in the semester and if you do poorly on any one of them kiss your grade goodbye. Her lectures are strange and she teaches things that aren't even history expecting us to recall movie characters and such. Quiz ?'s are absurd and if you miss a lecture it's over. Beware.
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Just here to verify all the other reviews. I took her this semester and she actually made me love history. She makes every time era in history interesting and fun by providing cartoons, having us watch movies, and more. If u take her, GO TO EVERY CLASS. Exams are heavily dependent on lectures but she's also hilarious and fun. Enjoy!!!

GREAT professor.  Watching a million lectures are annoying though because oyu have to take notes for your quizzes which you can use notes.  Make sure you rememeber all the quiz questions because she reserves the right to use past quizzes as her final. The two papers aren't bad, those were graded by the TA's and you have a rough draft.

Pamela Robbins is personally a really nice lady. Though if you are not good with history, her class is not for you. History is a really bad subject for me, yet I saw her rate my professor's reviews and gave this class a chance anyways. This was a mistake since I still struggled a lot with the information in this course.

Take Professor Robbins if you can!  Her online course relied heavily on PowerPoint presentations but she made the material feel so much more interesting than one would think. She teaches enthusiastically and super funny. Very important to take notes, they are basically written out on tests similarly. She gives two papers and drafts beforehand too!

Professor Robbins teaches real history from a critical race theory perspective and is one of the best professors at FSU. I would highly recommend taking her class. I think I'm a better person and a better American after learning the truth of American history from her.

Professor Robbins is absolutely hysterical. Attendance is not mandatory but to ensure a good grade on quizzes, go!!! The material is interesting, has clear grading criteria, and it is just a great class.

Dr. Robbins is incredibly kind and a great professor. The material is very interesting and her lectures are engaging and funny. Attendance isn't mandatory, there are five quizzes, a paper, and a final. Take this class if you are able to keep up with long lectures and notes. Great class to fulfill the W and Y diversity requirements.

Professor Robbins is an amazing teacher and human. She is so caring and funny which makes class enjoyable to attend. If you generally keep up with the lectures and score well on the tests you'll have a good foundation for final essay and exam. Find a good group of students to study and review assignments with. I wish she taught more classes.
One of the best teachers at FSU. She is kind and funny and she makes her lectures enjoyable. This class is lecture heavy, but it is a very doable class. I would 100% take again.

Pamela Robbins is the funniest professor you will ever take. She cares about her students, she is understanding and kind, and her lectures are very interesting and fun to watch. This class has been a real joy and if you are considering her as a professor, I highly recommend you do

Professor Robbins has been one of my favorite professors in college. She is the sweetest and actually makes the class enjoyable. You are graded on five online quizzes, a paper, and the final. Quizzes are straight forward, attendance is not mandatory but go. She talks a lot more in-depth about the material in class which is helpful for the quizzes.
reviews.rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: TAKE THIS CLASS! Dr Robbins is easily the best professor I've had at FSU. Lectures are recorded and watched at home & discussions during the class time. The lectures are a lot but they are manageable and her jokes throughout make it worth the listen. Tests are online (MC, T/F) & are exactly like the lectures. Take good notes & u will do good here

Dr. Robbins is super fun, honest, and understanding. You have to watch the online lectures and be in class because she discusses things that aren't in the lectures. If you show up and put in the work, she makes it easy to pass.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Professor Robbins is, by far, THE BEST professor I have ever had. Not only is she incredibly woke, she is fantastic at lecturing without making history boring. On a personal level, she is very relatable and hilarious and she will always get back to you via email, and she gives great feedback. This class, AMH2097, is my favorite class ever.

Easily the best professor I've had at FSU thus far. Her lectures and discussions are extremely enjoyable and funny, and she gives very specific information to help you on the quizzes! Make sure you show up to class, because sometimes she will lecture in-person on things she does not cover online. Highly recommend Dr. Robbins!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: I love this professor. I would give her a 5 if it were based on personality; however, all the lectures you have to do at home before you show up to classlots of lectures. One big paper consists of an outline, draft, and final. Your TA grades you. Few quizzes. You have to take good notes in class or the video lectures to pass the quizzes.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Dr. Robbins is an amazing professor. You do have to watch the lectures and take notes and I highly recommend attending class because she covers things that aren't mentioned in the assigned lectures. She is extremely nice, funny and makes history lectures enjoyable. She has a clear grading criteria and never assigns an unrealistic workload.

Prof. Robbins is so funny! She's the first professor I've had who genuinely makes me laugh and enjoy class. Lectures are engaging and informative. Make sure you actually attend her in-class discussions, as there is a lot of information on the quizzes that is only covered in them. She is very accessible and a great professor!

I took race and ethnicity in the Spring online and she was a very interesting prof. Her lectures were easy for me to watch because she teaches it as if we have no knowledge and spoke very casually. Write down everything she says, literally everything and youre golden.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Hilarious

reviews.comment: Very easy class, quizzes were sometimes hard and basically the only grades. Just watch your lectures and write you immigration letters and you'll be fine. This was asychronous so I don't know her well but she was nice.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Robbins was super sweet and even though there are a lot of lectures, there's no zoom or textbook readings. Don't wait until the last minute to watch all of them, the people who did that were the ones who complained the most about grades/workload. Her lectures are actually really interesting and engaging and every interaction I had with her was good

Her class is only graded upon seven quizzes and two papers, which is really nice. Her lectures can seem intimidating, but if you spread them out and take adequate notes you will be fine. Time management, following directions from the rubrics, and reaching out to Professor Robbins or your assigned TA will for sure get an A!

She is an amazing teacher! She gave the most interesting lectures and you can tell that she is passionate about the subject. I learned a lot in this class! The tests are easy if you take notes and pay attention. I also enjoyed the writing assignment. Overall, I would definitely recommend!

Dr. Robbins has been my favorite professor since I started college!! She provides you with lectures that are very informative and entertaining. The most important part of this class is watching the weekly lectures, which help you write your papers. She is extremely funny and so kind. I definitely recommend this class!

This class was remotely taught by Robbins and Mooney. They were very kind, and even gave me full credit for a late assignment, which was nice. Due to the lectures being asynchronous, they were a bit boring, but not too bad. The work is easy and graded generously as well. Just make sure you're keeping up with lectures as they're released.

reviews.comment: Probably the best professor I've ever had at FSU. Hilarious and makes lectures entertaining and worth your time. I always looked forward to going to class. Grades are super fair and not work-heavy. Only thing you have to do is take notes. Tests and papers were all fair and if you study you'll be fine. Funniest, kindest woman ever! Take her for sure

Professor Robbins has an amazing class, I usually hate history but having her made me change my mind. You have to go to class and write everything she says down but she is hilarious and gives extra credit on each exam. The papers aren't hard because she tells you what to write in class. Definitely take her ASAP!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Great professor, makes the subject interesting and shes so funny. 2 exams and a non cumulative final along with 2 papers. Exams are worth more than the papers, so focus more on that. Exams are multiple choice but really hard. Doesnt give extra credit or curve your final grade. Doesnt post notes online. Go to class & study hard and youll do well

reviews.comment: Best teacher at FSU. Makes history interesting and fun. Attendance taken from questions answered on notecards.
I absolutely loved going to this class. She is one of the best professors I have ever had. I definitely could have done better if I studied more. If you have the opportunity to take her, DO IT!

Dr Robbins was the best professor I've had at FSU hands down. I took this class as my required history, but I never expected to be so intrigued and engaged by the USA's immigration history. reviews.comment: We had two professors for the class and they'd switch off lecture days, so was always so excited when it was her turn. I thought it was going to be an easy A but she is a tough grader but as long as you follow the rubric you're fine.

Her lectures are entertaining & shes funny. She cancelled class three times before our second test & we had to read notes online. No one was sure what she was going to test us on. Half of the class failed. Tests make up most of your grade so write down everything she says. Her tests are easy when you take them but you could end up w/ a low score.

She is so funny and teaches you about perspectives on history you actually care about rather than just lecturing dates and boring events. She doesn't ask about dates. You have attendance tickets every class and 4 content assessments.

You have to go to her lectures because she doesn't post notes anywhere else, but her lectures are absolutely fantastic! She cracked me up during every class. This is one of the best classes I've taken at FSU with one of the best professors- I highly recommend!
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: BEST Professor at FSU hands down. Robbins is amazing and actually gets you interested in a subject that is hard for most to find interest in. The way she teaches engages you and its almost like listening to a funny store as she lectures

My favorite class this semester. She's so funny, tries her best to make things relevant and really knows the material. Best professor I've had so far at FSU. It's really important to go to class and I even got really bad FOMO when I didn't bc I loved the course so much. She's hilarious when she lectures and very competent/passionate in course content. I'd recommend her to anyone in a heartbeat. Went out of my way a semester later to write this bc she's so great!

Dr. Robbins is by far the best teacher I have ever had. I hate history but the way that she taught it, you will never forget. She is honestly such a great professor! Yes you have to go to every class, but it was one of those classes that you wanted to go! She makes the lectures so interesting! reviews.comment: Don't listen to the people who say the exams and paper are too hard. She gives amazing notes and those are the only resources you are allowed to use, so attending class is a must. Those people obviously skipped a lot or suck at writing. Great lecturer and makes class super interesting. Make sure you have great full notes

Go to class every day and take good, detailed notes or you won't get an A. There is no textbook or Powerpoints online to look at. It all depends on going to class and taking notes. Also, put extra effort into the immigrant comparison paper and the final exam. Otherwise, this class is entertaining, especially for a history class.

LOVE HER!! Best teacher at FSU. Super funny, interesting, and approachable. I'm not a history person, but this was hands down my fav class! Attendance isn't graded, but you need to go to get notes because she doesn't post them (no txtbook). Only 4 grades, so you don't have room to mess up one assignment; make sure to study. Tests are pretty fair.

Dr. Robbins is hilarious, but make sure you study. I would have done better if I took an extra day or two. Essay grades were fair except that TA who graded mine was wrong with occasional parenthetical citation placements (of which I would cross reference with the MLA handbook). I looked forward to coming to her class.

Class is pretty entertaining and not too difficult. Three tests which are pretty straight forward based on notes and movies. One paper spread out into two parts that some had difficulty with, but was not really too difficult. She also offered extra credit too help out.

Pamela does a great job at lecturing. She is very clear on what she wants you to know for the exams. Her exams are tough but if you study you should be fine. There is one paper that is split into two stages. She makes the class interesting and engages the students. She's also very funny and makes light of some of the sad topics discussed

Great professor! She is hilarious and always has a positive attitude. The only homework assignments given is to watch movies. Go to class and take notes because it is important for the tests. She has 3 tests throughout the semester.

Go to office hours before exams! Like a review session. The way she asks questions is challenging, a lot of comparisons. Lecture is very interesting and requires a lot of note taking. Great class! Requires work for the tests, not just knowing the notes. Had 3 tests and 3-4 short papers, 1 long.

I LOVED PROFESSOR ROBBINS! She is by far the best teacher I have had. She makes learning the material so much fun and is constantly cracking jokes during lectures. She assigns two tests (midterm and final), one paper, and has about 5 points worth of in class assignments. You need to show up and listen to lectures, but I would definitely recommend!

Loved her! Made me excited to go to class everyday!  Definitely have to be a good note taker to do well. If youre up for the work and up for a comedy show twice a week take AMH with her! Normally I go to my classes and just study to take the tests and then forget it all but I actually learned something! I laughed and learned at the same time ayyy
