Test choices A,B,C,A&B,all of the above when every group is the same. She will say every wave is the largest. I study hours on hours for the exams never miss class and get a 64 on them.
I thought she was great. I really wish I could take her class twice. I went to class and easily got an A. Loved her.
Great Professor and gives awesome lectures. However, there are hardly any grades in the class and the midterm and final are extremely hard.

I took this class because I thought it was going to be an "easy A". Turns out it's by far the hardest elective class I have taken at FSU. There are BARELY any grades. Only TWO exams, one paper, and 4 measly 5 point in-class assignments. She gives zero extra credit which was depressing. TAs grade paper extremely hard. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS

She is hands down the best teacher I've ever had. She is hilarious and very relatable. She makes what could be a boring class into something extremely interesting. The tests are a little tough, and tests are based solely on lectures because there is no textbook to use. Would HIGHLY recommend taking her class or having her as a teacher if you can.

The best class I've taken at FSU. She is an amazing professor and makes you actually want to go to class. Her tests are really hard but if you go to class and study you will do fine. Seriously a great class, I'd take it again if I could.

One of the best teachers I've ever had. She explains everything very thoroughly and makes class interesting and engaging. She is super funny and genuinely wants everyone to do well

She is a WONDERFUL lecturer and makes class very interesting! I loved going to class and learned a great deal in her class. However, her exams are very difficult. I attended class every day, and studied hard for her exams and struggled to keep a high B.

Dr. Robbins is a fantastic teacher. Her tests can be challenging, but she is extremely clear in lectures and has plenty of humor to go alongside the material. She is extremely passionate about the subject, which proves that she is one of the best professors at FSU. Take this class!
Best Professor I have ever had! She makes the material interesting and wants you to do well in the class. I highly recommend taking her!

Great teacher, Class itself is not mandatory but you must go to get the notes. Study the notes on the test and it isn't to bad. Three tests and two take home essays. This class is terrible.. if youre taking it as a grade to raise your gpa or to merely weed out your gordon rule that FSU requires..do NOT take her..again DO NOT. take Sheppard instead.. I have a 3.7 GPA and have never entered a test thinking i know the material just to find out i get a 68..ive never gotten a C in my life and this might be the first
She's the best

There are 2 essays and 2 exams, so you have to work hard to get good grades, but it is easy to make it to this class because she's so fun. I loved her & this class.

Great teacher, essays are autobiographies, 3 tests really easy, one test has an essay but she gives you the prompt a week in advance, made class fun and interesting even at 8 in the morning. loved the class.
one of the best professors at FSU!

This was honestly the best teacher I've taken at Florida State so far! She is so funny, and really turns a boring class into something special. I have heard mixed reviews about this course is I would get her as I have heard the class can be hard, however with her she makes it easy

AMAZING TEACHER! so funny! cusses a lot though so if you're offended by that dont take her class. As long as you go to class, take good notes, and study, you'll do fine. Great professor.  She keeps you involved and interested in the material with her colorful personality.  A lot of Exam questions come from her lectures, and she is not bad on the eyes, so make the effort, come to class, and you can easily get an A.

Funny professor that made the class very interesting. I was dreading taking a history class because I hate it with a passion, but after taking this class I LOVED IT! She is great. I would take any class with her again.
eeee

a wonderful person. the end.

I found the class to be engaging and interesting as well as well structured. You can tell Dr. Brooks enjoys teaching this class and appreciates people taking it!
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--So many papers

reviews.comment: Dr. Brooks may not be the most exciting lecturer but I found this class to be particularly well structured. The book used was excellent and had a lot of useful examples and problems to work. The tests were for the most part straightforward and fair. Would definitely recommend doing all the suggested homework and reviewing the lectures.

This course builds off of EEL4021 heavily. If that class put you to sleep, please prepare yourself for this one. Again, sit on the right-hand side of the class if you want to see the board. If you ever have any questions, however, the professor's office hours are almost always open. Never be afraid to ask him for help.

This course is very different than his usual classes but still makes it a signals-based statistics course. The beginning isn't bad but try to avoid drifting off towards the end of the semester. The material ramps up and his teaching style has a tendency to cause people to have heavy eyelids. Amazing and caring professor, though.

Professor's teaching style can be difficult, but this course is very simple compared to the pre-requisite. Sit on the right-hand side of the room if you want to see the board. This professor really cares and overall wants the best for the student. Office hours are abundant and flexible based on needs, he is always there to help.

This course is definitely a weed-out class. Make sure to pay keen attention, which can be difficult given the early class times and the professor's teaching style. Sit on the right-hand side of the room if you want to see the board. Despite any teaching difficulties, this professor really cares and overall wants the best for the student.

Often hard to stay awake in lectures but they are all posted online and have tons of useful information. Will not succeed on tests without doing all the homework and lots of outside studying. Professor is always around to help and answer questions.

Dr. Brooks is very willing to go out of his way to help any student. Material on tests is either covered in class or in homework assignments. Good grades can be easily achieved in his classes by coming to lecture, and putting in a little work outside of class.

If you show up to class you will know exactly what to expect on the test. Homework is not graded but helps for studying. He is more than willing to help you out if you show up to class on a regular basis.
difficulty staying awake in lectures, very boring, doesn't explain well
Style of grading is ridiculous. Have a page of work correctly done and type the very final equation in your calculator wrong? minus 80% of the points at least.

This professor is a may be a good person, but he is lacking in his abilities to relate material to students that are unfamiliar with the subject matter. This tends to be an issue when you are being instructed on material that is new. I actually noticed an improvement in my grades when I attended fewer classes. Could that be a sign of something?
Good person, but a terrible teacher. Do not know how to explain the material at all.

horrible instructor! Does not care about his students and even seem to hate them.His tests are really hard and long. Would be good if at least let people know that his tests are way harder than anything covered in class or in the book including HW.
Horrible teaching skills.  No useful examples.  No thought through, worked problems.  Horrible handwriting.  Doesn't care about students, just his job.
Cagle works hard at making research accessible and comprehensible without oversimplifying. Clear, friendly, and a fast, fair grader.

Cagle is the best teacher at FSU PC. He is fair, reliable, and completely devoted to teaching social work. He's also hot and really nice, so what more can you ask for?
Human sexuality--didn't learn much more than I already knew!  Memorizing alot of details in the book and tough tests AND 10-15 page paper for this elective!

One of, if not the best teacher at FSU.  Chuck cares A LOT about his students. I had the pleasure to take drawing and performance classes with him, the latter is his own area of interest. I went into performance thinking it wasn't that legitimate of an art form and it would be an easy grade but came out loving performance. His hair is great too.

I absolutely love Chuck. He is incredibly helpful and really cares about the progress of his students. He is also highly entertaining. I spent a lot of time time in his classes laughing at his hilarious anecdotes and witty wordisms.

He keeps students interested and classes engaging through humor and neat assignments. The music he plays during drawing sessions is also tip top. If drawing isn't really your thing he always has interesting ideas, but he mostly likes to sit back and see what you can come up with  especially in the later classes, when you're given near total freedom

He was WONDERFUL. I hated computers before this class but now I am really interested in Photoshop and other applications of the computer in art form. He has inspired me to use different media in my artwork as well as incorporate humor. Fantastic teacher!

He absolutely made the class, he had a great personality and always had helpful critiques. I learned so much that I can apply in many areas of art. Assignments were usually very engaging. I highly suggest taking a class with him!

Professor Calohan is an excellent professor. To be honest, she does not baby you or give you a ton of cushion during the semester, BUT she is precise, direct and on point with her power points, assignments and class activities. I probably did learn the most I have ever learned in one class from her. She is stern but with a purpose-to make u better!

I had her for Social Work Practice and Chemical Dependency.  She gives meaningful assignments and uses very helpful class exercises.  Exams are not easy, but if you put in the effort you can do well and learn a lot.  She is very fair with grades and actually takes the time to read your work and give meaningful feedback.
Great Teacher, I never get tired of sitting in her class or get sleepy in class. She keeps you interested by including activities
Interview and recording...She is a great teacher. Not easy but you will learn the material. She keeps it interesting

Prof Mike is an amazing instructor. His class at first was very surprising because it was a humanities class with the topic of Queer Theories.. never thought I would have stayed in the class but i am glad I did. Mike is hilarious and his classes are always a blast! Be prepared to laugh at all the jokes!! MAKE SURE TO READ! QUIZZES!

Meh, he's ok. Not my favorite professor but he's good overall. I took him at FSCJ. Just expect to do a lot of research for your speech topics, its basically like writing an essay and reading it with action. Like I said, meh. I would rather take Kuyper. No textbooks.

PROFESSOR MIKE IS JUST SERIOUSLY AWESOME!! THE BEST PROFESSOR I HAVE EVER TAKEN HANDS DOWN!! The speeches were actually fun,  and they were only around 5 of them. HE IS HILARIOUS and very clear to the subject. OH and BEING IN CLASS IS MANDATORY, Don't Be Lazy and DON'T SKIP THIS CLASS!! Anyways, take him, it's an easy A and you'll have fun!!!
My best teacher ever  take him class is very fun he is YOUNG really funny and interesting lectures there are three speeches  and thats it :)  YOU WILL LOVE THE CLASS. SAD that i  won't see him again.

Mike was amazing! Hands down best professor I have ever had. Class goes the whole time but lectures are so interesting that time flies by. There are only three speeches and not much other work besides that. He is so funny and a great person... Definitely recommend!!! (He's teaching at FSCJ now)

Best professor I've ever had! I did not want to take speech but he was so funny and kept class interesting I wanted to go! He only assigned 3 speeches, group work and some really easy essays! If you talk to him he is very understanding if you have to work etc as long as you participate and communicate with him! Highly recommend!

Currently teaching at FSCJ. Professor is super chill. He's really nice and knows that when speaking his students are uncomfortable. I enjoyed being in his class. If you put fourth even a little effort into your speeches you'll most likely get an A. Just do the little work he asks and practice a few times. Easiest A by far. Make sure you go to class

I was dreading taking a speech class and got so lucky to have found professor Chouinard. He only assigned us with three speeches and five papers with the option to write a 6th paper to drop the lowest grade. He made this class a lot of fun and enjoyable. Basically if you show up on time and try you will pass. Gives great feedback with his grades.
He was a really nice guy and an easy A. Doesn't expect much. He's so nerdy its kinda cute! haha
She is easily the best professor I have had so far. She is very helpful in workshop and in office hours. She is funny and super easy to talk to. Only 2 papers, journals, and a final project.

She's really easy to pass her class as long as you show up. I ended with a straight 100 in the class. Although her class was an easy a its not to say i didn't learn anything. She is very helpful when trying to develop your writing style, something that will stick with you past the pilimary classes.
Really helpful with your papers in workshops. Highly recommend taking her class.
She was a great english teacher. I have never liked English, but she made the subject enjoyable. All of the essay topics were interesting. I would definitely recommend her.
SO nice. Funny and wants her students to have a good time. Had her for 1101 and 1102 and would take her if I had another english! She is awesome! :)
Such a good teacher, so nice. Easy, fun, good essay topics and  makes class easy to go to.
No Comments

I had to take Dr. Marsh as a result of transferring with an AS instead of an AA -- At first I was very unenthused with taking yet another writing course but quickly became enamored with it. -- Dr. Marsh is a very kind and caring woman who has a way of instilling passion in even the most listless students! Be sure to give her your best efforts!

It is an easy A and you will literally love her. It is really not a heavy work load. There are three major projects in the class and small journal entries, nothing to stress about. She is always willing to help or give feedback and answers emails quickly. She explains how she wants each assignment done.

Fantastic teacher and human being. One of the best, sweetest, and most enjoyable professors I've ever had. I usually hate english and writing, but loved her class. She does everything great. I can't say enough about her as a teacher and a person, especially not in this limited amount of space. Seriously, take her class if you have the opportunity!

Down to earth, caring, and well-rounded professor whos book is about to hit the big screen. She has 2 decent sized essays and one final project which can be anything you chose. As long as you dont make grammatical mistakes, her grading is straight forward.

Mrs. Rios is a treasure.  Easily, one of the most caring and kind people I have known.  Her passion for education (particularly ESOL) is absolutely contagious.  She is wonderful as a person and as an educator.  She will change your way of thinking!

She is one of the most helpful and caring professors that I have ever experienced. She loves what she does, and she inspires her students to accept all of their future ESOL students. She even calls her former students to check up on them. An A+ Professor!

Her classes are a little light on actual invaluable teaching knowledge and learning the skills to help students. She is a very nice person, and certainly loves what she does. However, I enjoy classes that are less about the idealized fluffy  teacher stuff and more about raising the dismal literacy rate in America.
She has a passion for what she does, and a very kind heart. One of the best teachers I've ever had.

SOOOOOOO NICE! She really loves what she does. Although her directions are a little foggy at times she is always around to help. Her assignments are not difficult but are meaningful and useful as a future teacher. One of the best teachers I have ever had!!!!!!!!!
Awesome professor. Really enjoys what she does! The best I had!!!
Super helpful!  Great and informative class.
I took his political parties and campaigns class. He gives only essay tests but he tells u exacyly what will be on it. He is helpful and offers ectra credit. I'd take more of his classes if I could

Dr. Crew is very strait forward and direct, and people misconstrue that into what appears to be an "awful" perception of him.  He is extremely helpful and a very fair grader.  There are no party lines drawn here...they just come out in conversation.  I would take another class with him if I had the chance.
He was awful.  Gives grades based on party affiliation.
as long as you go to class and take notes, your good!

small curve at end of semester, no extra credit this class and the prof sucked. he barely tries to teach anything, and the exams and hard for no reason, you also only have about 1 to answer each question which was hard!! weekly discussions were also graded harshly. AVOID if you can, I would definitely not recommend him.

(Organizational Behavior) Literally the worst class ever. It was all online and the professor never posted a single video or lecture. The test covered 6 chapters (hundreds of pages) and no study guide was given. Tests are super hard, had to know unimportant small statistics. Graded the DBAs hard without any rubric.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: The online class is a breeze if you know how to study for the exams (not proctored, but time restricted to about 1.5 min/question). Type out his lectures and know key terms from the chapters. Lots of article questions on exams, none on quizzes. Easy A, very little busy work.

Douglas gets a bad rep because of his online classes, but I had him in person and his lectures are actually interesting and talks about real world issues that we can relate to. If you have him in person, GO TO CLASS and participate. Huge part of the grade was attendance. First test is very hard but he makes them easier as you go on
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: PLEASE DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS! Prof. Douglas teaches possibly the worst class I have ever taken in my academic career. Quizzes are too specific, sometimes referencing one single sentence in the textbook that does not seem relevant to the material. He sends out TONS of pointless emails. His lectures are not helpful. PLEASE do not take this class.

Took this class as a graduating senior that needed a filler 3000 level course. Please heed all these warnings and do NOT take this professor/class. Quizzes and discussion posts barely contribute to your grade while the two exams make up the bulk. DOES NOT allocate time evenly either; gives 15mins for 10q quiz but 60mins for 60q tests. MAKESNOSENSE!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Clear grading, just very test heavy.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Wow this class is the worst. Im not even through yet, but my grade dropped two whole letter grades because of one test that I got a C on. There are two tests both 30 percent of the total grade and the rest is BS busy work. The test was super hard and this guy isnt interactive with his students. DO NOT TAKE. I may drop.

It's very surprising that Dr. Douglas teaches an MBA course. The online course "lectures" feature him reading from Power Point slides and offering no professional context or insights. Exams include questions about incredibly arbitrary material. He seems to grade papers without actually reading them. A strange course but easy to make grades.

Pretty easy online class. All the answers to the quizzes are online BUT NOT THE ANSWERS TO THE EXAMS. 2 exams w/ many questions from the articles he posts each week. Didn't get the book and i didn't really need it.

It was my 10th class in MBA program and he was the 1st incompetent professor I had. Such an amazing topic like organizational behavior was ruined by him. Extremely boring lectures, exams' content is not covered anywhere (not in textbook, lectures, papers). He should be kicked out from MBA program  he is ruining reputation of FSU!!
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--TEST HEAVY

reviews.comment: At the core, the course is easy with weekly DBs and quizzes that generally can be found online. He is virtually available when you post your question on the PUBLIC discussion board in your course shell, but generally won't respond to personal emails. Tests are harder than quizzes, but there's a reason for the short time limit; take advantage of it.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--TEST HEAVY--Tests are tough

reviews.comment: Cesar Douglas is by far the worst professor I have experienced so far. This class really should be easy and interesting but his teaching style is just too awful. He speaks so slow that I have to put the lectures on 1.5x speed and his two exams do not provide enough time to do well. Do whatever it takes to not have this professor.

so disorganized and never gets back to you with any questions you have. His weekly quizzes are 20 minutes but you can't even go back and review your answers before submitting.....I skipped a question to come back to it so I didn't waste time and lost the points anyways because I couldn't go back. Very annoying.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: I took the online class and it's easy but be prepared to pretty much teach yourself everything. The tegrity lectures aren't related to the quiz material. Be prepared to read and take notes in order to pass the quizzes. Also,  its great quizzes AND tests  are open book but you have to know the material because you have very limited time.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: This class should be an easy A, but Professor Douglas is very unhelpful.  Tegrity lectures are short, but don't pertain to questions on quizzes/test.  However, all quiz/test questions can be found online.  Make sure to read articles carefully, questions on the test seem like they're meant to trick you. Only grades are 2 tests and quizzes.

This class was extremely easy. Prof Douglas doesn't do much though. You have to post any questions on a DB and he doesn't respond to emails. Basically just a quiz and DB every week. He doesn't give you any feedback and there are no guidelines to the DBs at all. Quizzes/tests are all from banks online. I never used the book, tegrity vids are short.

One of the easiest classes I have ever taken at FSU. He wants you to post on the forum instead of email him with questions which is kind of annoying but other than that a mostly nice guy and a very easy class. Tests are taken at home and not in the testing center.
Boring and snarky. Not approachable. Would not take again.
Awful professor. Non-responsive. References dated material and articles that are entirely irrelevant. Unfortunately, in the online MBA, there is no other option.

Dr. Douglas has a real problem with forming questions that accurately reflect the issue he wants the student to discuss. His writings are riddled with grammatical errors. His course format is disorganized.  This professor was a real disappointment.

Organizational Behavior - I found that the more I BS'd in my homework the better the grade I received.  You have to learn what it is that he wants to hear - so no developing your own opinion.  Just stick to rewording his notes and articles that he provides you.  Don't bother asking he questions - he dances around giving a clear answer.
Organizational Behavior - essay exams... your opinion can be wrong... I didn't like the tests at all, although he was a very nice guy.

This class can get a little in depth in subject, but as long as you complete your homework you will pass! He is quick to help, email and response times are quick, grades posted within a day or two, and extra credit sessions are given. Term project is 20% of your grade, but it is peer reviewed do you are not likely to do poorly. Amazing Professor!

Hinz is an amazing professor! Very helpful and knowledgeable in Finance. Enjoys teaching and is inspirational to his students. Homework is not mandatory, but test are pulled from the homework questions. You do the homework and understand it, you will pass!

He's a good guy that really gets finance. The class was kind of hard, but Professor Hinz was always more than willing to help you if you didn't get something. You don't have to do the homework, but the test questions come straight from it, so it's a great study tool. If you ever have an issue, definitely let him know. He gives extra credit too!

Always willing to help and will go out of his way for you. Homework isn't mandantory, but he randomly selects all of the test questions word for word from the homework, so it benefits you to do it. Really understands finance. Great guy!
Very great prof!!
I really really hated my time in her class. Her exams don't really reflect the material learned and she is an extremely tough grader. Absolutely do not take this class/instructor if you can avoid it.

I was nervous based on reviews but they couldn't have been more wrong. 5 DB, 3 exams, and a term paper. She was very generous grading the DB/paper and curved the exams quite a bit. Would definitely recommend !!

Great professor! Im actually glad I took this class and didn't get scared of the reviews. She actually curves a lot and her class is pretty easy. With the discussion boards I got 10s on all of them all you need to do is follow the rubric to the T!

Assignments are rough. The highest grade I received was an 8. I did everything she asked and could never make a 10. Exams are okay, just study the powerpoints. There's an end of the semester paper that was just really confusing. I would suggest taking another professor if you can.

Overall this class was pretty standard. 5 discussion boards, 3 exams, and one paper. I took this class during the COVID19 epidemic and she changed our last exam to a paper which she graded very leniently, and for that I respect her and would take her again.

She is a good teacher but it is hard to understand what she says, her tests are pretty difficult, and the term paper is just awful to have to write because there are so many complex instructions. BUT she is always there to help if you need her. Pop quizzes throughout semester so don't be late or you'll miss it and she will not go back over it.
reviews.rating_tags: BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--EXTRA CREDIT--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: EDIT: I wrote one of the reviews below and she has since sent out an email to the class apologizing for her TAs tough grading. I realize that we shouldnt take that out on her but she said she would work with us to fix this! Otherwise, the work is fair but it is somewhat difficult to understand her audio lectures until you get used to her accent.

Don't even try on your discussion boards because no matter what the highest you will get is a 6 or 7 out of ten. Change prompts hours before it is due and then get points off if you have already submitted it early with the original. For the tests take notes from the audio on the powerpoint lectures and memorize literally everything from that

Don't take this class online unless you want to write an essay for your discussion board that is worth ten points, because you'll end up receiving only 6 points anyway. Tests are tough and the class average was in the 70's and 80's, she said this was good.
Tests are basically straight from lectures. Discussion boards are graded pretty tough.

Required class, but she teaches quantitative research there is supposedly another teacher who will do qualitative research. Wasn't the worst thing I ever took and made me almost interested in doing research.
Awesome.  He works to make sure students understand the materials and are prepared to take a job.

great professor. If you're a RE major, He's your guy. The final RE investment group project seems like a Private Equity pitch, and is great practice. Definitely know your TVM finance material and basic RE principles and you should do fine. If you pay attention in this class you should be in a great position to move into real estate finance roles.

He is one of my favorite professors I've ever had!! He is so kind and cares so deeply about the success of his students. He is incredibly knowledgable and will do anything to help a student in need, whether it be in the class or career guidance!
Awesome.  Clear, concise and to the point.  Loved his class and the material.

Ive got a 2.8 GPA and thought that this was one on the best real estate classes.  There was 1 test and 1 quiz and almost the same as the study guide.  Final was easy.  10 times easy than the others that teach this class.
No Comments
Good professor.  Knows the material. Follows the textbook. Cares about students. Not an easy A.

She's a great lecturer and exudes an energetic passion for history. Very good communicator and more than willing to give help if you want or need it. If you actually go to class most of the time and take notes you'll do well. The paper's aren't graded very harshly and she gives extra credit for attendance whenever it rains. Tests are fairly easy.

Her tests (midterm and final only) were difficult because she gives lectures with just pictures on her power points, no words.  However, the essays weren't horrible.  You basically get to choose your topic.  She is very helpful if you go to office hours and she will give you suggestions to get a good grade.

Do not take this lady. It takes her until the end of the semester for any grades to be posted. She only has two exams in the class that don't match her study guide. Before one exam she said "oh I made this exam last night there's probably a lot mistakes but good luck" Then after the exam doesn't give you credit for her mistakes.

The biggest waste of time in my entire 20 years of my life.  This lady has no freaking clue on how to teach a class.  I warn all of you reading this NOT! to take her class.  Trust me, you will regret it.
Great professor and great man.  First professor to believe in and push me.  I remain in his debt and I will never forget him.
This man was part of every socialist/communist revolution in one way, shape or form and the most interesting and informative professor I have EVER HAD.
South American Politics, Marxist thought, Amazing insight because he has lived through it.
Dr. Dunnigan is one of the most helpful professors I have ever had. He is caring and if you take the initiative to ask questions you will be rewarded because he so knowledgeable.
Yeah, a little moody, but he definitely knows his stuff.
No Comments

He is a good professor, although he tends to favor the girls.  Most of the time his classes are good.  He likes to touch people, not in a dirty way, but he hugs and pats people a lot.  If you need help, just talk to him, he will try to help you.
Not a great teacher but is a pretty cool person outside of the class structure.
cool guy. Not too much interested in what he did cuz I wasn't into the band stuff...more orchestra...but he still cared, and taught me what he knew.
Agree on the moody thing.  He can be uber cool and then have his panties all up in a wad.
good teacher, a little moody and sometimes hard to get a hold of or talk to, but a good guy and very knowledgeable and fun!

Dr. Wynot not only cares about the subject matter he is teaching, but also cares deeply about his students.  He is personable and asks repeatedly, "any questions?"  He thrives on interaction with the class, which makes him an excellent professor.  Would gladly take another class from him.  A+!

His class was really interesting and enjoyable. I would recommend only buying one of the textbooks (Modern China). Grades are from mid-term and final which he gives you a study guide with what to study. Great Professor! :)

2nd time I've taken Dr.Wynot. Had him for Cold War and WWII. He is my favorite professor. He is so knowledgeable and interesting. He's a great story-teller. His lectures are never boring, really knows his stuff. He shows awesome videos to supplement lectures. I have learned so much in his classes. Gives helpful study guides for exams
one of FSU's best history teachers I've seen so far.  Knowledgable and easy to follow.  Good speaker and well prepared lectures.
Good professor, a recommendation to take if class is needed.
No Comments
No Comments
GREAT teacher!!!!  Extremely informed and helpful!!!
one of the coolest teacher's i've ever had.  i think some of the girls described him as a big teddy bear.  i highly recommend him.
Great Class!  Kept if fun, but very interesting.  Highly recommended, even if the class is not required.
He is one of the best professors I had at Florida State University.

A very nice professor and very willing to help you.  He will even guide you along on the tests if you are stuck on a problem- you just have to ask.  This class was more difficult that QMB 3200 with him.  Neither was particularly hard, but he can be hard to follow because he is very, and I mean very, boring.  But nonetheless, friendly and helpful!
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments
Financial Management of the Firm. Need to study and go to class, otherwise he's difficult.
He is seriously a lovely human being. He has you make connections to real life from your reading. Seriously fun prof.
I really liked his class. He's a laid back prof. and real funny. He has great insight into writing. Definitely take him.

Garriga is the man. He is a funny guy and really tries to be your friend rather than a teacher. He is really really flexible and works with you to make sure you do well. You write a few papers but they aren't hard and you have plenty of time to work on them. TAKE HIM!
No textbook. Chooses some great readings. Amazingly helpful if you need it which you probably won't. Not difficult, very personable. Would suggest taking his class. :)
i had him for physics studio and loved him! you can tell he loves physics and enjoys teaching. who doesn't love a russian nuclear physicist in an argyle sweater, anyway? take his class

Physics I is all Newtonian, so if you have trouble with the material its not his fault. He has a heavy Russian accent, but clearly speaks English. Grading was fair, class was a lot of work. Leaving a comment here just because he is not a bad professor at all.
he's not hard, he just doesn't fit academic environment and hey! are professors required to have some basic english to teach at FSU???????????????
the worst professor i have ever had at fsu! do not take this him unless your gpa can afford a C. all other ast1002 professors are really easy.. this one, is impossibly hard.

Speaks softly but clearly has been in his field for a long time. Don't let his age or soft spoken lectures fool you. This guy knows his stuff, and I suspect he is well-versed in the basics like English, math, and general scientific theory.  He grades fairly. If you read the material and pay attention you will most likely get an A.
One of the most amazing professors I have had the honor of learning from. Knowledgeable, respected, kind, interesting. You will learn so much from this great man.

I have had several classes with Dr. Murphy.  He is a great teacher and loves to communicate with his class.  He has very interesting discussions, especially on ABA.  He has a great background of knowledge and stories that he brings to every class.  You will learn a lot from his class. Short quizzes every week---no big exams to study for!!
Quiz every class, teaches like you should already know the answers, and the only way you can miss a class is if you have a doctors note, period.

Doctor Halvorson is an amazing professor! She cares about her students and goes above and beyond for them. She is always ready to help and gives great advice. I highly recommend her. Definitely take a course from her. She's the best!

Dr. H is a gem & a truly lovely lady, but frankly isn't a very good teacher. She does great at making herself available for feedback and clarification, but tends to ramble in her lectures and often leaves you wondering "what's your point?" Her grading is laughably easy which makes for an easy class experience, but I don't feel I've learned anything

She understands that students have lives outside of class and will work with you on what you don't understand. She genuinely cares about her students and wants them to do well. She doesn't make you feel bad when you make mistakes and is great at helping you find things you did well.

I genuinely can say that this was one of the most awful classes Ive taken. I was told this class is such an easy A. However, I had 4 different TA's this semester and I could not name one thing that was clear about grading or when things were due. I probably could have gone an A in this class if it was more organized.

Dr. H is a very vibrant professor. Graduate School is known to be stressful but taking her class was a breath of fresh air. I love the way that she build relationships with her students and gives great feedback. Her passion shines through. It made an early Friday class not so bad after all
Dr. H. is one of the best professors I've come across in my college experience. She is super passionate about her work and emphasizes on having discussions in her class. Would definitely reccomend

Dr. Halvorson is one of the greatest professors that I have came across.  She is very charismatic & influential. She has made the subject very interesting as she has employed new & interactive ways of teaching which is way more effective than traditional lecturing.  She has treated us like grown-up graduate students which is very important to me.
She is a great professor; although, she can be a little unclear of what she expects on assignments.

Dr Halvorson is such a wonderful professor. She is incredibly sweet and her classes are always interesting. She does give you homework every week, but it isn't just busy work. Everything she makes you do helps your grade a lot. I always sign up for her first. Super funny, helpful, and energetic!

She exemplifies all that is COMMUNICATION! She is the first instructor I met at FSU-PC and she's caring and never too busy to help a student. Take her courses if you REALLY want to LEARN amd grow!  She's AWESOME!!!

Very engaging class. Really wants and makes sure students find interest in subject matter. If you come to class, and do the work as outlined it can be a very easy and entertaining class. Learned so much and had a blast!!!

Dr. H was my first teacher at FSUPC. Needless to say, she rocked my world! She's always enthusiastic and willing to help you out with classes or advising. I am taking her again next semester. She's a sweetheart and outstanding professor!!!
She is AMAZING!!! Always willing to help and a total sweetheart! Her grading is fair and her classes are fun!! Take her!!! I'm glad I did!!
Dr. Halvorson is amazing !!! (that is all you need to know)

Professor Smith is the type of Professor youll always remember. He goes above and beyond for his students. He just wants everyone to succeed and takes into account that not everyone is a professional writer. Improvement is something he appreciates and looks for in ones writing so if youre willing to work with that youll pass the class for sure.
YES, HE DOES TALK A LOT BUT I LEARN A LOT TOO! KINDA FUNNY, HUH?!
He likes to talk! But it is good stuff!

Dr. Chanton is awesome. I've had him for 2 classes my whole FSU career and they were both two of my favorites. His lectures are actually interesting and he he really wants you to do well. TRULY. Even if you just want an easy A, you'll end up actually wanting to attend class. MUST TAKE.

Dr. Chanton was a great professor. He is always willing to help those who ask and is very understanding. I had some personal issues get in the way this semester and he completely understood and helped me out a lot. If he teaches any of your mandatory classes, you must take him!
What a great professor! A litte scatterbrained, but phenomenal. Very eager to help all students if you ask. I would definitely recommend him!

Professor Sullivan is great, the only problem with taking him during covid is that there are only 3 exams and MANY recorded lectures that you need for the exams. Even if you write down everything you find important from his lectures, you might still have a difficult time on his exams because they require critical thinking in a small amount of time.

This man is so sweet, he is one of my favorite professors at FSU. He was so caring and understanding during COVID, he handled it better than I could have hoped. You have to study to do well, but the Target Pack and class provide everything you need (don't buy the textbook). If you need more ratings there are some under his full name, James. Love!!!

He is hands down my favorite professor. He provides outlines of notes and you just take more on the side. Very easy A if you go to class. Attendance is not mandatory though. You do get extra credit by going to class.
Professor Sullivan is big gas. Summarizes the powerpoints into outlines that are awesome for test preparation.
Great professor! Lectures are straightforward and he is very clear about what will be on the tests.

Best professor hands down in my opinion. Take him for any class. Hilarious, great examples, inspirational, helpful, and tests aren't too hard. Assignments are easy if you pay attention. Very helpful with grad school and internships.

He was an amazing professor. He did not use power point but he lectured out of a target pack that was about $15. I did not read the textbook at all and made A's on all the tests. He knows a lot about the material because he has experience in counseling. He is willing to work with you if you show up to class and "pretend that you are listening."

Jim Sullivan is easily the best professor I have had at FSU. He knows the material and has personal experience to make it that much more knowledgeable. He does talk a lot but it's never dull- always interesting. Amazing class. Would definitely take again!

I took Dr. Sullivan for Psych of Personality and he was a great teacher. He is one of the very few teachers I love to listen to. You can easily tell that he loves his job and genuinely cares about his students. Usually he teaches with a target packet that he makes himself which follows the textbook. His tests are very fair if you study.
No Comments

love love love dr. sullivan! so nice and so eager to help if needed. he keeps his class interesting and does cdool extra credit assignments. he also teaches clinical and counseling which is also excellent. i would recommend him to everyone!

Im warning you now. Do not take Kogot if you care about yourself. Ive always had a high gpa but without the professor teaching, everyone in class was struggling to have more than a 40. You wont learn anything from the lectures, use YouTube and read the chapters because that will help more than any notes you take.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Loves to stand up in front of class and read off the PowerPoint. Doesn't like to do examples, when he does he usually gets them wrong. Assigns tons of optional homework. Does NOT grade with a curve.

This professor is the worst teacher I have ever had. I do not like insulting teacher's teaching abilities like this but never, ever take his class. Just use Mike Christiansen's youtube videos to learn Chemistry better than his useless lectures.

He gives a ten minute quiz every week. These quizzes consist of anything in the chapter you learn last week. Making it nearly impossible to tell whats going to be on it from the chapter. The HW isnt mandatory but highly recommend you do it to practice.

This mann is the best teacher I've ever had. He's super hot and knows his stuff. He is all about the more important things in life and really challenges you to find your inner self. Overall, he balls hard and I highly reccomend

I had him for 3 classes. He's tough and very artsy but he's a smart guy and pretty helpful with critiques.
